name: Adam Salisbury
bio: Adam wants to fight against obesity in America and help all children get healthier. He started STA with the goal of helping thousands of children learn to love exercising. He has six children of his own that are involved with the program and have been homeschooled for over five years now.

name: Adelaide Olguin
bio: Adelaide Olguin is the founder of TalkBox.Mom, a revolutionary program that empowers families to learn and speak a new language together naturally through their daily lives. With her immersive and practical approach, Adelaide helps parents and children break free from traditional language learning methods and start confidently speaking from day one. As a featured speaker at the Great Homeschool Conventions, Adelaide inspires families to transform their homeschool experience by incorporating fun, real-life language immersion into their routines.

name: Aimee Eucce
bio: Aimee Eucce is a second-generation homeschooling Mom. She beat the odds ... from a homeschooled kid who struggled with dyslexia, ADHD, and auditory processing issues to a college grad and successful entrepreneur. She now speaks across the country, inspiring and equipping homeschool families in all different stages of homeschooling. She brings a unique perspective as a pastor's kid who was homeschooled in the dark ages aka the '80s. You will laugh, cry and walk away inspired to tackle whatever challenges life throws at your homeschooling family. Owner of Play ‘n Talk Phonics, Spelling, & Typing curriculum.

name: Alice Reinhardt
bio: Alice Reinhardt’s extensive experience as a veteran homeschooler, entrepreneur, and mentor highlights her deep commitment to supporting the next generation of home educators. She and her husband, Mark, began their homeschooling journey in the “pioneer days” of the early 80s. Seven children and 35 years later, she is well past the questions of “is it legal” and “what about socialization”. As both new and seasoned educators seek guidance, Alice stands out as an invaluable resource, providing encouragement, wisdom, and a multigenerational perspective rooted in decades of experience and her passion for Christ. Believing you never stop learning, Alice recently returned to the classroom (2024) to complete her Master of Psychology degree to further develop her skills in advising home-educating families as they carve out their own path. Alice owns SpeakEasy, a family/life coaching and consulting business. You can find Alice at the Rhino Technologies booth (audio-visual provider for GHC).

name: Alicia Wood
bio: Alicia Wood is a dedicated homeschooling mom of four, entering her 15th year of home education. She has been married for 20 years to her high school sweetheart, Josh. A passionate traveler, Alicia has Italy at the top of her bucket list. She holds a Bachelor of Science in Secondary Education with an emphasis in Biology. Alicia volunteers as the West Region Director for the American Speech and Debate Association, where she enjoys mentoring new families and club leaders as they get started in the league. In her local community, she leads the Inspire Gilbert Speech and Debate Club.

name: Alison Agnew
bio: Alison is a wife and mother of four adult children, all of whom she homeschooled from kindergarten through high school graduation. During those years she served on the leadership team of her local homeschool co-op and homeschool athletic organization, taught many group classes, directed theater, and wrote for her blog, Alison Agnew Writes, where she developed a wide following. She now serves as the Student Engagement Coordinator at Portage Learning, an accredited, online institution that provides asynchronous courses for high school and college credit. She and her husband reside in a small town just north of Pittsburgh, PA.

name: Amanda Trumpower
bio:
Amanda Trumpower writes for Jesus lovers who dig dragons, detectives, and droids. She's a second-generation homeschooler, twin mom, and fan of all things nerdy. Her mission is to provide entertaining stories that make kids laugh and point their hearts toward God.

Amanda Trumpower writes for Jesus lovers who dig dragons, detectives, and droids. Becca Wierwille is the award-winning author of the Road Trip Rescue series and other stories that show kids they are wonderfully created for the unique adventures in their lives. They host a podcast called "Ice Cream with Authors: Christian Books for Kids," where they help families connect with stories.

name: Amy E. Barr
bio: Amy Barr is mother to 3 successfully homeschooled children, a Classical archaeologist who's excavated in Greece, Turkey, and Jordan, a popular homeschool blogger, and a co-founder of The Lukeion Project. She has developed the most extensive Latin program available to high-school students online, going 3 years beyond the AP level course. She specializes in ancient civilizations and Latin, with an emphasis on the Romans.

name: Andi Elliott
bio:
Andi Elliott has dealt with some of life’s most challenging times. Her daughter, Charlotte, was given a catastrophic diagnosis at just 1 year old, which turned their world upside down. Searching for answers, Andi became a full-time caregiver and advocate for Charlotte in the overwhelming world of complex medical issues. Charlotte’s ultra-rare disease demanded 24 hour care, and doctors gave little support as Andi navigated uncharted waters, all while managing her home and school. “Don’t believe the lie that God won’t give you more than you can handle”, Andi says, “I was not strong enough to endure what God asked of me, only my complete dependence on Him gave me the strength to get through each day.”
“God has shaped my perspective to live life to the fullest. He has taught me contentment in my situation, not apart from it. If he can bless me richly despite my circumstances, I know he can do the same for everyone.”
Andi lives in Mid-Central Missouri with her family and focuses on homeschooling, speaking, and operating their family businesses, for which she has a passion.

name: Andrew Pudewa
title: Director
bio:
Andrew Pudewa is a renowned educator, speaker, and the founder of the Institute for Excellence in Writing (IEW), a globally recognized organization dedicated to teaching students of all ages how to think, speak, and write with clarity and confidence. With a passion for fostering effective communication and critical thinking, Pudewa has transformed writing education through his innovative, structured, and engaging methodologies. A homeschooling father and classical education advocate, he has inspired countless families, educators, and students through his workshops, resources, and incredibly popular workshop sessions at events like the regional Great Homeschool Conventions – which draw attendees from 38 states.

Pudewa’s journey in education began with a diverse background that shaped his unique approach. After earning a Bachelor of Arts from the University of California, San Diego, and a teaching credential, he studied violin under the mentorship of Shinichi Suzuki and later served as an assistant to Dr. Suzuki in Japan. This experience instilled a deep appreciation for structured, incremental learning, which Pudewa later adapted to writing instruction. In the early 1990s, while teaching in California, he was mentored by Dr. James B. Webster, whose Structure and Style writing method became the foundation for IEW. In 1997, Pudewa founded IEW to share this method, blending classical education principles with practical, replicable techniques.

Through IEW, Pudewa developed a comprehensive curriculum that emphasizes clear thinking and elegant expression, used by homeschoolers, private schools, and public institutions worldwide. His flagship program, the Teaching Writing: Structure and Style course, equips educators and parents to teach writing systematically, breaking it into manageable steps while encouraging creativity. IEW’s resources, including student workbooks, online classes, and themed writing courses, cater to diverse learners, from elementary students to high schoolers, and even those with special needs. Pudewa’s approach, often infused with humor and storytelling, makes writing accessible and enjoyable, earning him accolades like the 2019 Homeschool Hall of Fame Award.

A highly sought-after speaker, Pudewa has presents at the five regional Great Homeschooling Conventions each year – with these events drawing attendees from 38 states! (Andrew also spends countless hours engaging one-on-one with attendees at these conventions)

Andrew’s Great Homeschool Conventions workshops cover topics like writing, motivation, and classical education. His talks, such as “The Four Deadly Errors of Teaching Writing” and “Reaching the Reluctant Writer,” offer practical strategies for educators and parents. Pudewa is also a published author and host of the IEW Podcast, where he discusses education, parenting, and the value of the liberal arts. His commitment to classical education is evident in his advocacy for memorization, logic, and rhetoric as tools for intellectual growth.

A father of seven, Pudewa and his wife, Robin, homeschooled their children in Locust Grove, Oklahoma, where IEW is headquartered. His Christian faith informs his educational philosophy, emphasizing the pursuit of truth, beauty, and virtue. Pudewa’s global impact includes training educators in countries like Canada, Australia, and China, and his resources have been translated into multiple languages. Through IEW, he continues to empower students to master the art of communication, leaving a lasting legacy in homeschooling and education

name: Andy Keusal
bio:
Andy Keusal is passionate about using his teaching gift to edify and strengthen the body of Christ by boldly teaching the truth about money, inflation, and banking. For the past 28 years Andy has trained commercial bankers across the United States, and this year he created Biblical Money, his first course for Christian families.
As a husband and father of ten children, Andy devotes much of his time and energy to helping his wife Andrea educate and disciple their nine children still living at home. Whether at home or in the workplace, Andy has a knack for making complex things simple, using humor and a comfortable presentation style to communicate with his audience.

name: Angela Smith
bio: Angela is a homeschool veteran of over 30 years and the founder of Inquisicook. She’s on a mission to turn curious students into intuitive cooks–not just recipe followers. She lives in Northeast Georgia with her husband, where she teaches co-ops and workshops. In addition to all things cooking and science, she’s passionate about nature study. The forest is her happy place, and she can often be found there foraging mushrooms, photographing tiny critters, and enjoying the beauty of Creation.

name: Barry Pettyjohn
bio:
For the last 15 years, Barry Pettyjohn Sr. has been in multiple ministries involving children. Van Ministry; Sunday School Teacher; Awana and Master Clubs; Youth Preacher; Junior counsellor at Summer Camps - all with the mindset to support the parents in raising their children in a godly manner.

Raising Christ-Centered Children is not a request from God, it is a command from God. Psalm 40:3 is his theme verse, which recognizes our role once we are saved. The Holy Spirit changes our hearts so that we can be a witness to others. And, through that witness, lives may be changed. Barry believe there is no greater place to be a witness than in the home.

Preacher and Teacher of Children and Adults since 2010. The teaching always emphasizes Christ and the ownership we must take in our relationship with Him.

I am teacher at heart. The thing that matters most to me: Being a messenger with the right message - the message of our Savior. Psalm 40:3 is my life verse "...He hath put a new song in my mouth, even praise unto our God: many shall see it and fear, and shall trust in the LORD."

I believe everything we teach our children has an impact on not just their acceptance of Christ, but also their willingness to share their faith with the world.

name: Becca Wierwille
bio:
Becca Wierwille is the award-winning author of the Road Trip Rescue series and other stories that show kids they are wonderfully created for the unique adventures in their lives. She also cohosts a book podcast for kids, Ice Cream with Authors: Christian Books for Kids 7-13.
Born with only half of her right arm, Becca aims to help others find beauty in what makes them extraordinary. Despite her dreams of sandy beaches and mountain peaks, she loves living in Pennsylvania with her family.

Becca Wierwille is the award-winning author of the Road Trip Rescue series and other stories that show kids they are wonderfully created for the unique adventures in their lives. Her short stories for children and devotions have been featured in numerous magazines and other publications.
Mom by day and novelist by relatively early in the morning, she wishes she could eat peach pie as often as most writers drink coffee. Despite her dreams of sandy beaches and mountain peaks, she loves living in Pennsylvania with her family.

name: Becky MacCredie
bio: Becky is a Certified Cognitive & Behavior Coach and Psychometrist at The Nectar Group. She received her Bachelor of Science in Early Childhood Education with an emphasis in Literacy and Special Education from Youngstown State University. She is a licensed teacher in Ohio, North Carolina, and Maryland. In addition to spending 14 years as a classroom teacher she has mentored new teachers, provided county-wide professional development, and worked with student teachers. She has also owned a childcare business providing individualized education to the children in her care. She loves making personal connections with her students and creating positive learning experiences for them. When she isn’t working with families, she loves baking, decorating cakes, camping with her family in their RV, and reading books. She enjoys singing and has even been a member of an opera company. Mostly she loves spending time with her husband, three kids, and their three fish!

name: Bill Nealey
bio: Bill Nealey Jr is the Executive Director of Mission To Haiti. Bill made his first trip to Haiti at the age of 14. As a young adult, he was called to serve with Mission To Haiti. At the same time he attended Miami Christian College and earned a degree in Missions and Intercultural Studies. Bill and his wife Sherril have been married for 37 years. They have three grown children who were homeschool and attended Christian schools.

name: Brad Pauquette
bio: Brad Pauquette is the bestselling author of The Novel Matrix, a groundbreaking novel-writing guide which has helped thousands of writers write and publish their best work. He is also the founder and director of The Company, a practical community for world-changing Christian writers. Brad Pauquette lives in Cambridge, Ohio, with his wife and seven children.

name: Brian O'Rear
bio: Brian spent a career as an Air Force instructor pilot. Upon completing his military career, he started a business that became one of the country’s largest home inspection companies, selling it in 2018 to pursue a doctorate in entrepreneurship. He continued guiding, coaching, and teaching small business owners and was an adjunct professor of entrepreneurship at Boyce College. He brings a mix of leadership, entrepreneurial, and coaching experience to help the students explore their full potential in the business world.

name: Brianna Cardiff Hicks
bio:
Chris and his wife Monica homeschooled their three daughters all the way through high school. Together, Chris and Monica founded the largest homeschooling support group in Silicon Valley, All Ways Learning, where they served for over ten years.
Chris also served for five years as the founding president of the California Homeschool Network, a statewide homeschooling organization whose mission is to protect the fundamental right of parents to educate their children in the manner they deem appropriate without interference by federal, state, or local agencies.
He is the author of a number of articles on homeschooling, including “The Seduction of Homeschooling Families,” “Toward An Educational Renaissance,” and “Making the World Safe for Homeschooling.”
Chris has served as an Elder at Faith Church Kingstowne since 2011; he most recently served as Executive Officer from 2023-2025.
Chris and Monica, with their daughter Brianna and son-in-law Steve, started the nonprofit American History Education Project in 2020 to help the next generation of homeschooling parents (and grandparents!) teach and share their values with their children. Together they developed a free American History curriculum built around Great Picture Books to help parents introduce their young children to the ideals and blemishes of our history.
Professionally, Chris spent most of his career in software engineering management. His last 12 years he served as Chief Operating Officer for i360, the leading provider of political data and technology for Republicans.

name: Bryan Davis
bio: Bryan Davis is the author of several fantasy/adventure series for youth and adults, including his bestselling Dragons in our Midst series. After twenty years as a computer professional, he shifted to writing and now has more than fifty books in print. He and his wife, Susie, have seven adult children, and they work together as an author/editor team.

name: Carla Cox
bio:
M.A. Reading
M.A. Special Education / Learning Disabilities
Reading Specialist
Teacher (35 years)
Owner of LTR Reading Clinic (42 years)

name: Carol S. McCoy
bio:
Carol S. McCoy is the co-creator of Steadfast, a high school biblical worldview curriculum that integrates history, literature, writing, apologetics, and the humanities. She believes learning should be an adventure and that variety and creativity are essential ingredients for engaging students and producing truth-seeking thinkers and lifelong learners. One of her main goals in writing Steadfast was to provide a quality curriculum that is engaging and relevant while being user-friendly for both teacher and student.
Carol earned a B.A. in education and a minor in music from Oral Roberts University. It was while attending ORU that her passion for teaching was ignited. Carol taught in public schools for seven years before becoming a full-time mom. She then devoted herself to homeschooling her four children and teaching at a homeschool co-op for more than twenty years. Her greatest joys are spending time with her husband Mark and their four grown children, their spouses, and her growing brood of grandchildren.

name: Casey McCracken
bio: Casey McCracken serves on the Leadership Team for Christian Home Educators of Ohio (CHEO) and is a homeschooling mom of three sons, who keep their family in a constant - and joyful - busy season of life. She has a heart for helping families get started on their homeschooling journey and encouraging them along the way. Casey is passionate about reminding families that homeschooling is not about perfection, but about keeping Christ at the center of it all and faithfully walking out the calling God has placed on their family.

name: Casey Sadler
bio: Casey Sadler is the Senior Manager of Training at Lincoln Learning Solutions, bringing over 18 years of experience in education. With nearly a decade dedicated to professional development and training, Casey blends her background as an algebra teacher with a passion for empowering educators and families. She holds a Master of Education in Curriculum and Instruction from Clarion University. Outside of work, Casey is a proud mom of two, often found cheering on the sidelines as a spirited cheer coach and devoted wrestling mom.

name: Cassandra DeLeon
bio: Cassandra DeLeon is a pastor’s wife, mother of 10, and has enjoyed homeschooling her children for the last 17 years. She has enjoyed being a HomeWorks by Precept consultant for over 12 years. Cassandra loves drinking coffee with friends, serving with her family in ministry, and teaching ladies’ Bible studies. She regularly speaks at conferences and women retreats. Her greatest joy is teaching God’s Word to ladies of all ages and walks of life (theonething.cc).

name: Chad Parish
bio:
As the president of third generation Schukei Inc., operations manager of Ready Lamp LLC, and a lifelong entrepreneur, Chad brings over 25 years of business ownership experience to the table.
In addition to their current businesses, Chad and his wife, Cheri, previously owned two strength and conditioning facilities in Colorado. Through these facilities, Chad coached more than 55 top-five state finishers, over 15 Colorado state champions, and more than 25 collegiate athletic scholarship recipients. He was also recognized as the Colorado High School Activities Association Assistant Track & Field Coach of the Year in 2015.
As a father of three homeschooled children, Chad applied his coaching expertise to his oldest daughter, Keely, guiding her to a top-three national ranking in discus during her high school years, later becoming a two-time All-American and finishing second at the national level.He again used his coaching skills to guide his son, Kevin, to achieve a third-degree black belt in Tae-kwon-do. Having reached such an achievement, Chad then used his business savvy to help lay the groundwork for Kevin to open a highly successful martial arts dojo when Kevin was just fifteen years old and instructing all age groups.Chad and Cheri continued on with their youngest daughter, Kari, guiding her as she took home the title of 2022 Miss Teen Wyoming. They have assisted Kari in hosting multiple large scale community events and supported her growth as an aspiring Christian musician. Graduating high school a year early, she has recently released her self-produced debut single, “Isn't Over Yet” at an age of only seventeen.Their most recent venture, “Civil Dispute,” is a game created by his wife, Cheri, then graphically designed and digitally marketed by Kari, taking it nation-wide and showcasing it in two of the nation’s top toy shows.Chad seeks to educate families on what they are capable of when they come together to help one another in the pursuit of their goals, mainly entrepreneurship. He promotes the concept of parents guiding their children through the practical operations of running a business where they learn real-time how to work in the world around them.

name: Charla McKinley
bio:
Charla McKinley graduated from the University of Texas with a degree in Finance. She went on to become a Certified Public Accountant with over 25 years working in both the corporate and private sectors. While homeschooling her two children, Charla was inspired to write an interactive personal finance curriculum that opens the student's eyes to the high costs of being an adult. After retiring from homeschooling, Charla continues to teach teens live in Raleigh, NC and across the country using her Beyond Personal Finance curriculum.

Charla’s passion is teaching teens that their choices matter. She is a firm believer that in order to prepare teens for the road ahead they must be given the opportunity to practice making good (and not so good) choices using real dollars before they get out into the world and have real regrets.

name: Charlotte Prinkki
bio: Charlotte Prinkki, M.Ed., is a holistic health educator and curriculum writer. As a full-spectrum doula and teacher, with multiple specialized certifications, Charlotte has worked with children of all ages and spent many years mentoring parents through challenging stages of family life. She brings a compassionate, research-informed approach to helping families build confidence, access practical resources, and foster lifelong healthy habits. Charlotte loves learning something new daily, and is passionate about: having “informed consent”, coffee, Jesus and sharing tips that make life easier.

name: Cheri Kay Sessions
bio:
Cheri Kay Sessions (PT, DPT) has 17+ years of experience as a pediatric physical therapist, special needs advocate, educational consultant, and homeschooling mom. Cheri Kay’s four amazing children were all adopted and have a variety of unique needs.

She received her bachelor’s degree from Michigan State University in Athletic Training, a Master’s Degree from the University of Rhode Island in Physical Therapy, and a Doctor of Physical Therapy Degree, with an emphasis on pediatrics from Southern California.

Along with her training as a Physical Therapist, she is also trained to provide trauma-informed care and is fluent in American Sign Language. She has experience in navigating the school system, the community mental health system, and the health care systems as both a parent and a professional. With her unique understanding of how these systems work, she is equipped to advocate for families that also need to access these services. She has a unique perspective of the needs and desires of homeschooling families with her variety of experiences as both a parent and a professional.

Cheri Kay enjoys traveling and has spent three years living overseas in the beautiful country of Haiti, serving the Haitian people through various medical and therapy clinics. Along with parenting the children who have stolen her heart, Cheri Kay’s passion is to come alongside other families with resources and encouragement to help them soar into their own success.

name: Chiara Sandri
bio:
Chiara Sandri
Chiara Sandri is a practicing Catholic, a wife, a mother, an author/illustrator and a pharmacist. She was born and raised in Italy, where she graduated in Pharmacy School and subsequently earned a Master in Bioethics. After working as a pharmacist for several years in Italy, she moved to the United States with her family, where she became a California Licensed Pharmacist. The teachings received during the years of the Master in Bioethics have confirmed and strengthened her scientific, ethical and religious belief that every human life has to be respected and cherished from the moment of conception to the moment of natural death.
In recent years, her innate passion for writing and drawing has led her to create and publish fiction and early learning books for children. “With Every Beat of My Heart” is her first Christian non-fiction book for families, a voyage into the miracle of the creation of every new human life. She currently lives in Maryland with her husband and their three children. She volunteers as Treasurer at Frederick County Right To Life, for which she also designs pro-life educational material. Her favorite time is the one spent with her family, her source of infinite joy. Each day she is deeply grateful to God for every single blessing in her life, even for the hard days, during which she can experience even more His neverending love, help, support, forgiveness and mercy.

name: Chris McKenna
bio:
Chris McKenna: Founder, Protect Young Eyes, and Executive Director of the PYE Foundation. Chris is a respected digital safety voice, creating valuable and practical resources for families and schools. His impactful 2019 testimony before the US Senate initiated a series of hearings to further online child protection policy. Years of gritty effort earned PYE the 2020 Dignity Defense Award from the National Center on Sexual Exploitation and the 2024 Child Safety Award from The Phase Alliance. Recent state-level legislation co-authored by Chris - the Child Device Protection Bill - passed in Utah and moved through multiple chambers in six other states. It will force devices to enable existing content filters when activated by a minor. He is a technical advisor to Angel Kids AI and works closely with digital safety advocates and legislators to craft policies that reign in abusive technologies. As a representative of the Human Change Movement, Chris spoke at the 2024 World Economic Forum. Under his guidance, the Protect Young Eyes team conducts presentations worldwide to parents, professionals, and schools, and gained recognition by being featured in the Childhood 2.0 movie. Their website, courses, and private parent community show caregivers step-by-step how to prepare and protect their children in digital spaces. Chris cherishes family, including road trips with his four children, connecting with nature, running, romping with his pups, and almost anything sweet.
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/prot...
LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/ch...
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/prote...
Website: https://protectyoungeyes.com/
2 sentence version: Founder of Protect Young Eyes, author, and leading digital safety advocate, advising
legislators and emerging tech companies. Featured in the Childhood 2.0 documentary,
while leading a team that educates parents and schools worldwide on digital safety.

name: Christian Hauer
bio: Christian Hauer, author of The Bygone Sagas of Oogerel and The Marvelous Mr Motley is a writer, inker, letterer, and self taught graphic designer. Growing up as a homeschooler, he became eager to complete great projects, like writing (two) children’s books, building a disc golf course for a local park, and earning the rank of Eagle Scout from the Boy Scouts of America. As a result, he has learned to wear many hats and to diligently work on a project each day until it has become a lifestyle for him, not just for his comic book series and companion Biblical Reflection Guides but also in his walk with Christ and his industrial career. When he gets a moment of free time he enjoys adventures with his family, video games and of course, reading comics.

name: Chuck Black
bio:
Chuck Black, a former F-16 fighter pilot and tactical combat communications engineer, is the author of twenty-three novels, including the popular Kingdom Series, The Knights of Arrethtrae series, the Wars of the Realm series, The Starlore Legacy series, and Call to Arms: The Guts and Glory of Courageous Fatherhood. Kingdom’s Dawn of The Kingdom Series received Homeschool.com’s “Voted #1” award and was on CBA’s top ten best sellers list twice in 2008 for all Christian Youth Literature. His heart is to equip and encourage families in their pursuit of Christ, and he seeks to do so through his allegorical and Scripture-based novels, his seminars, and his published articles.
Chuck has served as an Elder at Cornerstone First Baptist Church in Williston, ND and has taught adult Sunday school classes for the past nineteen years, including frequent class sessions on parenting. In addition to speaking at homeschool and Christian conferences all across the United States and Canada, he has written columns for the Teach Them Diligently Convention and the North Dakota State Homeschool Association. Chuck has also been published in LifeWay’s Home Life Magazine, The Old Schoolhouse Magazine, Walk Thru the Bible’s Stand Strong Magazine, and on various websites and e-newsletters. He is also an entrepreneur with sixteen patents and is currently the president and general manager for FlowCore Systems, a chemical injection automation company in the oil and gas industry.

Chuck is a believer in Jesus Christ as Lord and Savior and in the Holy-Spirit-inspired, infallible Word of God. He is devoted to his wife, Andrea, and their six children and multiple grandchildren. Chuck and Andrea homeschooled their six children for twenty-four years; all six have graduated from college and are married. Chuck’s passion is to inspire people of all ages to follow the Lord with zeal and to equip parents, pastors, and youth leaders to accomplish the same.

name: Clare Bonn
bio: Clare Bonn graduated from Cal State Long Beach with a BA in Sociology. She has worked for 22 years with school-age children in childcare, recreational classes, and classroom settings. She taught in a seated classroom for 3 years utilizing Blackbird and Company curriculum, and currently is a an online tutor for the company's Pages classes. She believes every child’s voice should be valued and every unique gift cultivated. Because every child is differently abled, she believes we teachers must meet the needs of each individual. Clare has three differently-abled children who all learned to be exceptionally authentic readers, writers, and thinkers using Blackbird and Company curriculum. You can find Clare during her free time in the garden or reading a good book.

name: Craig Dehut
bio:
Craig Dehut is a documentary filmmaker, media consultant, Bible class teacher, podcast host, and Co-Founder/CEO of Appian Media, a non-profit organization that creates videos from the Bible lands. Their award-winning docu-series and other media are freely available in over 160 countries. Craig grew up in a homeschooled family and is a homeschool dad, living in the Indianapolis area with his wife, Karen, and their three children.

Craig Dehut is seasoned documentary filmmaker, video producer, and media instructor deeply passionate about creating content that leaves a positive impact on our culture and inspires the next generation to share well-crafted stories. In 2016, Craig co-founded Appian Media, a non-profit media production company that produces award-winning documentaries and other resources from the lands of the Bible and makes them available to the world for free in order to re-engage people with the power of the Bible.

name: Cyndy Bonds
bio: Cyndy Bonds is a Conference Specialist who exhibits and presents home education workshops for Easy Grammar Systems. Her workshops are based on the principles of Dr. Wanda C. Phillips who has made grammar easy and enjoyable while ensuring that students achieve mastery. Born and raised in Arizona, Cyndy graduated from Arizona State University in 1976, Magna Cum Laude. During her twenty-year teaching career, she used the award-winning Easy Grammar texts in her various classes.

name: Dale Cox
bio: Dale Cox is a professional food scientist (B.S., M.S. Food Science) with 23 years’ experience developing and optimizing foods, many of which you will recognize. Now, he brings a unique perspective to how we should eat, including processed foods, to his Edible Knowledge brand of award-winning* food science experiment-based courses, including an online only series for the whole family designed to improve your cooking and baking skills and demystify mystery ingredients through food science. An avid fan of physics and LEGO bricks, he also has created popular physics courses that use LEGO Technic elements for the experiments. *2024 and 2025 Practical Homeschooling Magazine First Place customer choice awards

name: Dale Gamache
title: Founder and President
bio:
Dale Gamache “graduated” in 2017 from the public school system after 35 years teaching the Spanish language…⁄¡VIVA! …He taught elementary, middle and high school students in the Volusia County , FL school system including 30 years at Seabreeze High in Daytona Beach. Señor Gamache and his family established their own private Spanish language after-school program where they taught public, private, and homeschool children in the Daytona Beach, Florida area. They have instructed hundreds of elementary and middle school students in these after-school programs. La familia Gamache developed the LA CLASE DIVERTIDA DVD curriculum in 1999 while teaching these classes. Their program has reached tens of thousands of families World wide. He and his wife Robin have also developed a survival Spanish computer program in conjunction with the Florida Baptist Children’s Homes with a focus on missions for adults. Sr. Gamache instructs high school students all over the USA LIVE (NOT virtual) thru internet classrooms for HIGH SCHOOL SPANISH I, II and III credit.

Sr. Gamache has been a featured speaker on Foreign Language Acquisition at both homeschool and public school conventions throughout the country.

Dale and his wife Robin live in the Daytona Beach area and have one daughter, Erin and are blessed with three grandsons Jensen (7), Ean (4) and Jace (17 months).

https://www.funclase.com/

name: Davy Liu
bio: From The Lion King to Mulan, Beauty and the Beast to Star Wars, Davy Liu’s artistic fingerprint can be found in some of Hollywood’s most iconic films. As a former animator and content creator for Walt Disney Animation, Warner Bros., and Lucasfilm, he contributed to stories that have shaped generations. But while achieving success in the entertainment industry, Davy experienced a deeper calling—to use his God-given creativity not just for Hollywood, but for the Kingdom. Overcoming academic struggles as a child, Davy discovered his true strength in creativity. That discovery became the foundation for a lifelong mission: to inspire children and families with stories of faith, hope, and imagination. In 2004, he founded Kendu Films, a creative studio dedicated to producing original, faith-driven content for children. Through award-winning picture books, innovative curriculum, and animated films, Davy reimagines timeless Bible stories through the eyes of animals, helping young audiences connect with spiritual truths in fresh and memorable ways. Today, Davy is an internationally recognized speaker, author, illustrator, and CEO. He has taught at the Academy of Art University in San Francisco, spoken on TEDx stages, and shared his expertise with children of global leaders and artists alike. With a passion for equipping the next generation, Davy continues to create exceptional content that nurtures creativity, sparks faith, and empowers children to believe they, too, can make a difference.

name: Deborah Burns
bio: Your instructor has been a medical transcriptionist for more than 30 years, operates a large MT biz and has taught at colleges and conventions around the country. You’ll enjoy her warmth, humor and practical advice. Any questions? Contact Deborah directly: [email protected]

name: Deede Cauley, M.Ed., LPC, LMFT, FSAFBI
bio:
The Real Reading Company is known for providing outstanding curriculum for teaching reading, spelling, comprehension, and vocabulary. Deede Cauley, the presenter, is the author and creator of the phenomenally successful READING AND SPELLING PURE & SIMPLE series (RSPS). For nearly forty years her heart for struggling readers has led her to research, focus on what works, and teach reading and spelling to children, teens, and adults. Her experience included a teaching career that started in 1972 and provided her an opportunity to experience instruction at nearly every level (university, junior college, high school, middle school, and elementary). In the I980’s she became a charter member and officer of a local adult learn-to-read organization, and she had the privilege of homeschooling her son. Homeschoolers have been relieved and delighted that an easy-to-use curriculum with true phonics-based lessons is now available.

Mrs. Cauley’s RSPS curriculum integrates her Biblical values with her education and work experience that enable homeschoolers to easily produce excellent readers and writers:
• B.A. from SMU
• M. Ed. in education and guidance counseling from NTU
• Texas Licensed Professional Counselor (LPC) and Marriage & Family Therapist (LMFT)
• Former FBI Special Agent under the Reagan administration

name: Donnie Rosie
bio: Donnie Rosie has served at Miracle Mountain Ranch for 16 years, first as a student and now as Director of Marketing and Development. When he is not working for Miracle Mountain, he runs Rosie Marketing, his marketing and photography firm. His photography ranges from event and wedding photography to business shots and landscapes. He is married to his awesome wife, Tonya, and they have four children, George, Cole, Grant and Karis.

name: Dr. Carol Reynolds
bio:
An immensely popular and engaging speaker known for her infectious passion, humor and ability to connect music with history, Professor Carol is also fluent in Russian and German - with extensive research experience in both cultures.  

Dr. Carol Reynolds developed a fascination with Russian culture during her teenage years. She earned her Bachelor’s of Music from Hollins College, Virginia, followed by a Master’s degree in piano and a Ph.D. in musicology from the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill. Her doctoral research was conducted at the Leningrad Conservatory in the early 1980s, supported by a State Department grant, which deepened her expertise in Russian and Slavic studies. She then had a distinguished career as a professor of music history at Southern Methodist University (SMU) in Dallas, Texas. During her tenure, she founded and directed the SMU-in-Germany program, living and teaching in Germany multiple times since 1981.

After retiring from SMU in 2006, Dr. Reynolds and her husband, Hank, a music theorist and copyright attorney, moved to a ranch in North Central Texas, where they began raising goats. They also became involved in homeschooling and the revival of classical education. Under the brand “Professor Carol,” she started designing multimedia courses in history and the fine arts, focusing on making the arts accessible and meaningful through humor, substance, and skilled piano performance. Her signature courses include: 

  • Discovering Music: 300 Years of Interaction in Western Music, Arts, History, and Culture (2009) 
  • Exploring America’s Musical Heritage (2011) 
  • America’s Artistic Legacy 
  • Imperial Russia 
  • Early Sacred Music

Dr. Reynolds is a much sought-after speaker and study leader for Smithsonian Journeys, leading arts-focused tours to destinations such as Russia, Germany, Austria, Switzerland, Poland, Hungary, the Czech Republic, Croatia, Slovenia, the Baltic States, the Holy Land, the Mediterranean, the Caribbean, and across the Atlantic. She has also led tours for the Dallas Symphony Orchestra and Dallas Opera, and speaks at conferences, art institutions and events like the Van Cliburn Series, Dallas Symphony, and opera companies. Her engaging style, combining history, humor, and energy, has earned her praise from travelers, who describe her as insightful, approachable, and enthusiastic. She also contributes to the classical education movement, collaborating with organizations like Memoria Press and participating in initiatives like “Classical Education Unhinged” alongside other educators.

Professor Carol emphasizes the importance of Western cultural heritage, famously proclaiming, “The arts are not an elective. It’s a treasure!”

For more information, visit her official website at ProfessorCarol.com or check her Smithsonian Journeys profile at SmithsonianJourneys.org.

name: Dr. Chris Rusk
bio:
Dr. Rusk holds a master’s in English and a doctorate in education. He has worked in various teaching roles with learners of all ages – ranging from young children to adults.
He has a particular passion for working with international students in ESL settings.
As a homeschooling father of 6, Dr. Rusk has many years of experience working with non-traditional forms of education – giving him the ability to relate to many of the issues facing today’s students and their parents

Chris Rusk is the Dean, Superintendent, and Head of School for the Liberty University Online Academy. He has years of experience working with K-12 homeschool students and their families.

name: Dr. Christopher Rupe
bio: Dr. Christopher Rupe, president of Back2Genesis, holds a biology degree from SUNY Geneseo and a Ph.D. in geology (earth science) from Loma Linda University, where he specialized in sedimentary geology and presented his research at the Geological Society of America. Since his early twenties, Chris has spoken widely on creation/evolution across educational, media, and church venues. In 2009, he felt led by the Lord to start Back2Genesis, a non-profit ministry focused on creation apologetics. From 2011–2018, he served as a Research Associate at FMS Foundation, collaborating with former atheist and geneticist Dr. John Sanford of Cornell University on numerical simulations addressing Haldane’s Dilemma and the fixation of deleterious mutations. In addition to his research in the area of human population genetics, Chris spent several years studying the so-called hominin fossils, aka “ape-men”, and coauthored the book Contested Bones with Dr. Sanford. Chris has published numerous articles on topics ranging from pseudogenes and bacterial evolution to the origin of life and the fossil record.

name: Dr. Craig Wrobleski
bio: Dr. Craig S. Wrobleski is passionate about discipleship in the home, church, and beyond! He currently serves as the President and CEO of LifeStone Ministries. LifeStone Ministries has merged with Union Gospel Press, a publishing company that has been in business for 120 years. LifeStone Ministries produces Christian homeschool curriculum, as well as small group resources(lifestoneministries.org). Craig has a Master’s of Divinity from Liberty University and a Doctorate of Ministry in Discipleship at Liberty University. He and his family are active members of Living Rock Ministries.

name: Dr. Dan Sheffler
bio: D.T. Sheffler is professor of philosophy and academic dean at Memoria College and the author of Plato and Christian Personalism. He holds a Ph.D. in Philosophy and an M.A. in Classics from the University of Kentucky. He has taught philosophy, Latin, Greek, history, composition, and Great Books at colleges and classical high schools for 15 years. His research, writing, and speaking is wide ranging, including such topics as classical education, Christian Platonism, early Christianity, conservatism, beauty, and Western Civilization. His writing has appeared in the The Federalist, American Catholic Philosophical Quarterly, Quaestiones Disputatae, Touchstone, The Classical Teacher, and The Imaginative Conservative. He lives in Louisville with his wife Rose and three children, Ebenezer, Autumn, and Phineas.

name: Dr. Donald K. Egle
bio: Dr. Donald K. Egle is a recognized thought leader, speaker, and author in the higher education, business, brand marketing, and organizational leadership sectors. Egle’s proven and successful track record of cultivating the unique and powerful interchange between brand and organizational promises spans over two and a half decades. This work focuses on advancing, positioning, protecting, and defending organizational brands in the marketplace and building creative and influential teams of brand professionals. Currently, Egle serves as the Vice President for Enrollment, Marketing, & Communications and Chief Marketing & Communications Officer for LeTourneau University: the Christian Polytechnic University. At LeTourneau University, Egle leads a dynamic and award-winning team of enrollment, brand marketing, and communications professionals. Together, they steward one of the premier Christian higher education brands. As one of the leading brands, LeTourneau University serves as a broker for many of the diverse and significant aspects of the public discourse and innovation related to faith, science, and technology. Before he arrived at LeTourneau University in May 2015, Egle served as the Senior Director of Communications and University Spokesperson at James Madison University. For nearly nine years, Egle led a talented team of communication and public relations professionals who contributed to JMU’s accelerated ascent to a leading national public institution of higher education. Egle’s professional experience and contributions are further reflected in his time as the Director of University Relations for Liberty University from 2004-2006. Egle led a team of public relations professionals and served as the public relations lead for Liberty University’s Founder and Chancellor. In that capacity, Egle represented Liberty University and the Founder and Chancellor in news media outlets worldwide. Egle earned his Ph.D. in Organizational Leadership with an Entrepreneurial Leadership specialization from Regent University’s School of Business and Leadership. His dissertation focused on the topic of joyful leadership. He also earned his MBA and B.S. degrees from Liberty University. In addition to his academic credentials, Egle is Accredited in Public Relations (APR) by the Public Relations Society of America Universal Accreditation Board and an Accredited Business Communicator (ABC) by the International Association of Business Communicators. Egle is among a very small and prestigious group with both professional accreditations. In addition to his service and contributions to higher education, Egle is an entrepreneur and provides dynamic leadership, brand coaching, and consulting services to leaders in diverse industries.

name: Dr. Dusty Hess
bio:
Dr. Dusty Hess is a nationally recognized Functional Medicine Doctor, author, speaker, and founder of Upstream Health—a transformative movement helping Christian families reclaim their health by returning to God’s original design. Through his visionary leadership and resources—including the Upstream+ membership and Upstream Magazine—he’s raising a new standard of care rooted in faith, family, and functional medicine.
Known for simplifying complex science and delivering practical, gospel-centered insight, Dr. Dusty is a sought-after speaker at Christian health summits, medical conferences, and church-based wellness events. His message inspires families to break free from chronic disease, lead with purpose, and build a legacy of health and faith.

name: Dr. Jacquie Carroll, AFC
bio: Jacquie Carroll is the CEO of GameKraft, a small independent game creation and publishing company located in Palm Springs, California. After a successful career in higher education, instructional design, and student success, she built on her passions for teaching, learning, and having fun creating innovative educational games. Her first game, GET 9 is designed to help individuals break through their mental blocks around numbers and math. Get 9 is a way of making math, engaging, and enjoyable for all ages.

name: Dr. Jay Wile
title: Ph.D.
bio:
Dr. Jay L. Wile is a distinguished nuclear chemist, educator, and author renowned for his contributions to homeschool science education. With a Ph.D. in Nuclear Chemistry from the University of Rochester and a B.S. in Chemistry, Wile has earned multiple awards for excellence in teaching and research. He is best known for creating the Exploring Creation with… series, a cornerstone of homeschool science curricula, and later founding Berean Builders Publishing to produce engaging, rigorous science courses for K-12 students. A passionate advocate for homeschooling, Wile has influenced countless families through his textbooks, online classes, and speaking with us each year at our Great Homeschool Conventions.

Wile’s love for science was sparked by a childhood chemistry set, leading to a fascination with nuclear chemistry. After conducting research under the Department of Energy and National Science Foundation, publishing over 30 peer-reviewed articles, he transitioned to education, driven by a desire to make science accessible. In the 1990s, he authored the Exploring Creation with… series for Apologia Educational Ministries, covering biology, chemistry, physics, and more for junior high and high school students. In 2006, he sold the Apologia textbook business to focus on new ventures, later launching Berean Builders in 2011. His Science in the… series for elementary students introduces science through a historical lens, using hands-on experiments and a Christian worldview, with titles like Science in the Beginning and Discovering Design with Chemistry.

A committed Christian and young-earth creationist, Wile integrates faith into his curricula, addressing topics like creation vs. evolution in his books and lectures. His blog, Proslogion, explores science, apologetics, and homeschooling, often challenging mainstream scientific narratives, such as the age of dinosaur fossils based on carbon-14 data. Despite criticism from sources like RationalWiki for his views, Wile’s clear, conversational teaching style and practical examples have earned praise from homeschoolers, with students noting his ability to make complex concepts engaging and relevant.

Dr. Wile and his wife, Kathleen, married for over 35 years, homeschooled their adopted daughter, Dawn, from childhood through high school. Jay teaches online courses in chemistry and physics through Berean Builders, offering 32-week videoconference classes that blend textbook material with current scientific discoveries. A much sought-after speaker, he addresses homeschooling, science, and apologetics.

Based in Anderson, Indiana, Wile continues to innovate in science education, emphasizing critical thinking and hands-on learning. His Berean Builders curricula, lauded for their flexibility and depth, cater to diverse homeschooling styles, earning high reviews (4.5-5 stars on Christianbook.com). As an author of 13 books, a researcher, and a mentor, Wile’s mission is to equip students to explore science through a lens of faith and reason, leaving a lasting impact on the homeschooling community.

name: Dr. Karen S. Miller
bio:
I graduated with a doctorate in Theology in 2023 and a second Master’s degree in Christian Counseling in 2025. I have an MS in Reading & Language Arts and a BS in Elementary Ed. and Special Ed. My husband and I homeschooled our daughter from birth through high school and continue ministering to homeschool students by offering programs in Science – Hands on Electronic Kits and Curriculum/Courses for the last 15 years. New Book: Nobody is Talking about The Dead Elephant in the Room – How to Overcome the Fear of Death

Karen S. Miller has a Doctorate in Theology along with working on a masters in Christian Counseling. She has an MS in Reading & Language Arts and a BS in Elementary Ed. and a minor in Special Ed. She and her husband homeschooled their daughter from birth through high school and continue ministering to homeschool students by offering programs in Science – Hands on Electronic Kits and Curriculum/Courses.

name: Dr. Larry Schweikart
bio:
Dr. Larry Schweikart is a renowned historian, professor, author, and former rock drummer, celebrated for his engaging and patriotic approach to American history. Born in Mesa, Arizona, he grew up in a cattle ranching family in Higley before moving to Chandler after his father’s death in 1960. A graduate of Chandler High School, he earned a B.A. in Political Science from Arizona State University (1972), followed by an M.A. in History from ASU and a Ph.D. from the University of California, Santa Barbara. From 1985 to 2016, Dr. Schweikart taught history at the University of Dayton, specializing in business, economic, and military history.

A prolific author, Dr. Schweikart has written over 25 books, including the #1 New York Times bestseller A Patriot’s History of the United States (co-authored with Michael Allen), 48 Liberal Lies About American History, and Seven Events That Made America America. His work emphasizes a positive view of America’s past, countering what he sees as biased narratives in modern textbooks. In 2009, he produced the documentary Rockin’ the Wall, exploring rock music’s role in the fall of the Iron Curtain, which aired on PBS. His other works include Reagan: The American President and A Patriot’s History of the Modern World (with Dave Dougherty), and he has appeared on major media outlets like Fox News, Glenn Beck, and Rush Limbaugh.

As a speaker at Great Homeschool Conventions, Dr. Schweikart captivates audiences with his “Professor Larry” style—intellectually irreverent, humorous, and vivid—making history relatable and inspiring. His workshops offer homeschooling parents and educators practical tools to teach history with a focus on critical thinking, primary sources, and America’s founding principles. A former drummer who opened for bands like Steppenwolf, he brings unique energy to his presentations, blending storytelling with scholarly insight.

Dr. Schweikart resides in Chandler, Arizona, and continues to produce educational content, including video courses and webinars, to make history accessible and engaging.

name: Dr. Melanie Wilson
bio:
Dr. Melanie Wilson is a Christian psychologist, homeschooling mother of six, and author who transitioned from a clinical psychology career to become a leading voice in the homeschooling community. Residing in Missouri with her husband of over 30 years, Mark, Wilson has homeschooled her children for more than 20 years, with all six having graduated and pursued college education. Her work focuses on empowering homeschooling parents with practical tools for organization, sanity, and effective teaching, particularly through her innovative language arts curricula. A featured speaker at our Great Homeschool Conventions, Wilson inspires families with her insights on homeschooling, faith, and productivity.

Wilson holds a Ph.D. in Clinical Psychology and previously worked as a clinical psychologist, college instructor, freelance writer, and public speaker before feeling called to stay home and educate her children. This shift led her to found Fun to Learn Books, the publishing company behind her Grammar Galaxy series, a story-based, elementary language arts curriculum praised as a Cathy Duffy Top Pick. Launched in 2014, Grammar Galaxy uses engaging narratives to teach reading, writing, and speaking, keeping students motivated while simplifying complex concepts. In 2023, she published Fast Grammar, a story-based grammar curriculum for high schoolers, further expanding her educational impact. Wilson also authored The Organized Homeschool Life (2016) and A Year of Living Productively (2019), offering practical systems for managing homeschooling and family life, inspired by her adaptation of the FLYLady organizational method.

Through her podcast, The Homeschool Sanity Show, and her blog, Psychowith6.com, Wilson shares sanity-saving strategies drawn from her psychology background and homeschooling experience. Her podcast, lauded for its encouragement, covers topics like teaching multiple ages, managing homeschool chaos, and fostering emotional resilience. She also runs a YouTube channel (@melaniewilson). Her workshops at Great Homeschool Conventions, such as “Organizing Your Homeschool Life” and “Teaching Language Arts with Story,” provide actionable advice for parents, emphasizing efficiency and joy in education.

A devout Christian, Wilson integrates her faith into her work, viewing homeschooling as a calling that aligns with her commitment to family and God. Her family’s involvement in her husband’s library book business reflects their collaborative spirit, with her children often assisting at librarians’ conferences. In her free time, Wilson enjoys scrapbooking, playing tennis, and reading, passions that fuel her creativity. Her mission is to equip homeschooling families to thrive, combining her psychological expertise with practical, faith-based strategies to create organized, fulfilling educational experiences.

name: Dr. Rochelle Matthews-Somerville
bio:
Dr. Rochelle Matthews-Somerville is a distinguished special needs educational consultant, homeschooling advocate, and mother of six, renowned for her expertise in supporting families of children with unique learning and behavioral challenges. As the President and CEO of EFM Educational Consultants and Team Leader of the Special Needs Educational Consultants at the Home School Legal Defense Association (HSLDA), she has dedicated over 25 years to empowering individuals with disabilities to become confident, lifelong learners. Based in Charles County, Maryland, Rochelle, alongside her husband, Nate, has homeschooled their six children—five boys and one girl—for nearly 15 years, successfully guiding their eldest to graduate in 2021 and pursue mechanical engineering at the University of Maryland.

Rochelle holds an M.S. in Special Education, focusing on Autism/Asperger’s Syndrome and Behavior Disorders, and a Ph.D. in Psychological and Cultural Studies, concentrating on Learners with Severe and Communication Disorders. Her extensive career includes roles as a tenured university professor, K-12 resource teacher, county-wide autism specialist, and educational consultant across public, private, and homeschool settings. With over 20 years of clinical experience in early childhood and special education, she has developed mentor programs, facilitated school-wide assessments, and created individualized programs for students with diverse needs, including autism spectrum disorders and twice-exceptional learners.

Since joining HSLDA in July 2019, Rochelle has served as a passionate speaker and program director, offering practical strategies for homeschooling families, particularly those with struggling or neurodivergent learners. She directs Classical Conversations of Waldorf and is a sought-after presenter at conferences, delivering workshops on evidence-based methods to address communication, social skills, executive function, and motivation challenges. Her sessions, such as “You Can Homeschool Your Struggling Learner” and “Understanding the Twice Exceptional Learner,” provide parents with diagnostic checklists, curricula ideas, and multi-sensory teaching techniques to foster success in homeschool environments. Rochelle’s work emphasizes nurturing emotional regulation, empathy, and curiosity through tailored, sensory-friendly approaches.

A devoted Christian, Rochelle integrates faith into her educational philosophy, encouraging families to tap into their children’s God-given potential. Her family’s homeschooling journey, which spans kindergarten through high school, reflects her commitment to creating an educationally rich environment, inspired by her own upbringing with a teacher mother and policeman father who instilled a love for learning. As editor of the Meyerhoff Parent Association and a contributor to platforms like the Homeschool Highschool Podcast, Rochelle shares her expertise on topics like high school transcripts, student education plans, and fostering social-emotional development.

Rochelle’s mission is to equip homeschooling families with the tools and confidence to navigate challenges, from auditory processing disorders to complex high school transitions. Her work with organizations like The Autism Community in Action and Great Homeschool Conventions underscores her dedication to building inclusive, supportive communities. Living with her family in Maryland, Rochelle continues to inspire through her advocacy, offering hope and practical solutions to homeschooling parents worldwide.

name: Dr. Rosalie Hopkins OD, FAAO, FOVDR
bio:
Dr. Rose is a Board Certified Primary Care and Developmental Optometrist, and has been practicing in the Upstate since 2000. In addition to general optometry, she focuses on Pediatric and Developmental vision-care, and specializes in Vision Development and Rehabilitation. She sees pediatric and adult patients, including those with special needs and acquired and congenital brain injuries.

Dr. Rose completed her undergraduate studies at UC Berkeley, and then earned her Doctorate of Optometry from the Southern California College of Optometry. She completed her residency in Binocular Vision & Vision Therapy at the State University of New York College of Optometry, and then returned to her Alma Mater to teach optometric interns the applications of Vision Therapy in a clinical setting.

Dr. Rose is a Fellow of the American Academy of Optometry, a Fellow of the Optometric Vision Rehabilitation Association, and in the process of obtaining her Fellowship with the Neuro-Optometric Rehabilitation Association. She enjoys active memberships with the American Optometric Association, the South Carolina Optometric Association, the Piedmont Optometric Society, and the Optometric Extension Program.

name: Dr. Seann Dikkers
bio: Dr. Dikkers is a husband and father of two (graduated) homeschooled children. He currently works as Executive Director at Portals developing support for new homeschooling families. He has worked as a middle school teacher, high school principal, researcher, the Department Chair of Education at Bethel University, and authored books including TeacherCraft, Mobile Media Learning, and Real-Time Research exploring the powerful impact of play, design, and, *gasp* even having fun while learning.

name: Dr. Steven Coyle
bio:
Dr. Steven Coyle is the National Director High School Development with Universal Technical Institute. He is beginning his 47th year in education. He is retired from public education having served as a teacher, coach and high school administrator. He holds a bachelor’s degree in education, two Master’s degrees, and a Doctorate in Educational Leadership.
For the past 20 years, he has served in private education as an admissions representative, Director of Admissions, Director of Counselor/Academic Relations, and his present role. He has become a leading advocate for career and technical education having spoken at school counselor conferences, administrator conferences and national conventions all over the country. He also does webinars and podcasts for the Imagine America Foundation. He is heavily involved and a member of ACTE, ASCA, SkillsUSA, FFA, Alabama STEM Coalition, the National Association of Workforce Boards, STEM Ed Coalition, and TIVA Board of Directors. He has been asked to speak at several homeschool conferences across the country including the Great Homeschool Conventions. He was recently asked to serve on the Board of Directors for the Imagine America Foundation.

name: Dr. Tom Kemnitz
bio:
Dr. Thomas Milton Kemnitz is a distinguished educator, historian, and the founder of Royal Fireworks Press, a publishing company established in 1977 to create innovative curricula for gifted and talented children. With a Ph.D. and a background as a university professor specializing in Victorian England, Dr. Kemnitz grew frustrated with the lack of preparation he observed in his students for higher education. This led him to develop child-centered, rigorous, and creative educational materials that emphasize depth, breadth, and joy in learning—qualities that have become hallmarks of his work. His vision for education prioritizes art, humor, and intellectual engagement, making complex subjects accessible and inspiring for students.

When homeschooling emerged as a viable educational option, Dr. Kemnitz embraced it, recognizing its potential to offer tailored, high-quality learning experiences for all children, not just the gifted. Through Royal Fireworks Press, he has authored numerous books for children, parents, and teachers, including works on ancient Greece and Rome for the acclaimed Michael Clay Thompson language arts curriculum. His publications focus on fostering critical thinking, creativity, and a love for learning, making them popular among homeschooling families and classical educators.

As a Featured Speaker at our regional Great Homeschool Conventions, Dr. Kemnitz is celebrated for his insightful and passionate presentations, which empower parents and educators with practical strategies to implement demanding yet joyful curricula. His talks often explore the importance of pedagogy in homeschooling, encouraging parents to cultivate curiosity and intellectual rigor in their children. With decades of experience, he bridges historical scholarship and modern education, offering a unique perspective on how to prepare students for a lifetime of learning.

Dr. Kemnitz’s work continues to shape the homeschooling and classical education movements, with Royal Fireworks Press serving as a trusted resource for families worldwide. His commitment to enriching education through creativity and depth has left a lasting impact on countless students and educators. Learn more at RoyalFireworksPress.com.

name: Dwayne Walker
bio:
Dwayne Walker is a creative visionary, entrepreneur, and passionate advocate for nurturing creativity in the next generation. Homeschooled for much of his childhood, Dwayne discovered the healing and transformative power of art after his father fell ill when he was ten years old. That moment sparked a lifelong mission to help children use creativity as a tool for confidence, healing, and innovation.
At just 21, Dwayne cofounded Sparketh, an online art education platform that has since taught over 60,000 kids and teens in more than 100 countries. His journey, from a homeschool basement school to the Shark Tank stage, embodies the limitless potential of creativity.
Dwayne is also the author of Create or Die: 48 Keys of Creativity, a powerful guide to unlocking human potential through creative thinking. As a sought-after thought leader, Dwayne leads a global creative community called World of Creatives, uniting thousands of artists, writers, and thinkers worldwide. His YouTube channel, followed by over 50,000 people, inspires creators of all ages to embrace their imagination and build meaningful lives through creativity.

As a sought-after thought leader, Dwayne leads a global creative community called World of Creatives, uniting thousands of artists, writers, and thinkers worldwide. His YouTube channel, followed by over 50,000 people, inspires creators of all ages to embrace their imagination and build meaningful lives through creativity.

name: E. Calvin Beisner
bio: Founder, President, and National Spokesman of The Cornwall Alliance for the Stewardship of Creation (2005-present); former professor at Knox Theological Seminary (2000-2008) and Covenant College (1992-2000); former editor, Discipleship Journal (1993-1995). Author of (1) Social Justice vs. Biblical Justice: How Good Intentions Undermine Justice and Gospel; (2) What Can Theology Say to Science about Climate Change? (3) The Competing Worldviews of Environmentalism and Christianity; (4) Is Capitalism Bad for the Environment? (5) Prosperity and Poverty: The Compassionate Use of Resources in a World of Scarcity; (6) Prospects for Growth: A Biblical View of Population, Resources, and the Future; (7) Where Garden Meets Wilderness: Evangelical Entry into the Environmental Debate; (8) What Is the Most Important Environmental Task Facing American Christians Today? (9) Creation Stewardship: Evaluating Competing Views; (10) Biblical Foundations for Economics; (11) How Does the Creation Care Movement Threaten the Pro-Life Movement? (12) The Cosmic Consequences of Christ's Crosswork; (13) Answers for Atheists, Agnostics, and Other Thoughtful Skeptics: Dialogs about Christian Faith and Life; (14) God in Three Persons; (15) Psalms of Promise: Celebrating the Majesty and Faithfulness of God; (16) Evangelical Heathenism? Examining Contemporary Revivalism. Co-editor of Climate and Energy: The Case for Realism. Editor of Auburn Avenue Theology, Pros and Cons: Debating the Federal Vision. Ordained ruling elder in the Presbyterian Church in America and the Orthodox Presbyterian Church. Married to portrait artist Deborah Melvin Beisner (1983-present), father of seven, grandfather of 19.

name: Elizabeth Elliott
bio:
Elizabeth has a Bachelor of Science degree and have also pursued advanced studies in Neuro-Developmental Delay Therapy with the Institute of Neuro Physiological Psychology in Chester England (INPP), as well as becoming a Certified Listening Fitness Trainer with The Listening Fitness Center, Canada.

Her training with INPP and LiFT has allowed her to approach children’s difficulties and disabilities from a developmental perspective.
Elizabeth is a workshop speaker who has conducted workshops in several homeschool conventions across the country.
She is also a former homeschool mom who homeschooled both her kids all the way through high school. Both of them have graduated from college.
As the homeschool mom of a child who struggled with learning and academics, Elizabeth knows first-hand the pain, frustration and sometimes the helplessness that come with having a struggling learner.
If you are a parent seeking help for your child, Elizabeth knows exactly how you feel, she has been there. Be encouraged, there is hope!

Elizabeth has a Bachelor of Science degree and have also pursued advanced studies in Neuro-Developmental Delay Therapy with the Institute of Neuro Physiological Psychology in Chester England (INPP), as well as becoming a Certified Listening Fitness Trainer with The Listening Fitness Center, Canada. Her training with INPP and LiFT has allowed her to approach children’s difficulties and disabilities from a developmental perspective. Elizabeth is a workshop speaker who has conducted workshops in several homeschool conventions across the country. She is also a former homeschool mom who homeschooled both her kids all the way through high school. Both kids have graduated from college.

name: Erich Pratt
bio:
Erich Pratt is the Senior Vice President for Gun Owners of America, where he has been employed since 1990. GOA is a national grassroots organization representing more than two million Americans dedicated to promoting their Second Amendment freedom to keep and bear arms.
GOA lobbies for the pro-gun position in Washington, DC and is involved in firearm issues in the states. GOA’s work includes providing legal assistance to those involved in lawsuits with the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives, the federal firearms law enforcement agency.
Pratt has appeared on numerous national radio and TV programs such as NBC’s Today Show, MSNBC, CNN, CBS and Fox Cable News. His columns have appeared in newspapers across the country, including USA Today.
In recent years, Pratt has authored Firing Back: How to Counter Anti-gun Propaganda and Become a Better 2A Activist, a practical guide for refuting anti-gun arguments. Most recently, he wrote Armed for Liberty: A Student's Guide to the Second Amendment, a compelling, Scripture-rooted and history-rich resource that equips high school students to understand, defend, and appreciate the God-given Second Amendment right that safeguards all other freedoms.
Pratt has been involved with youth for over two decades, teaching civil government to high school students, serving as a Merit Badge Counselor in the Boy Scouts and coaching Little League baseball teams.
Mr. Pratt and his wife, Stacy, have homeschooled all eleven of their children.

name: Eve Hullette
bio: From the time she was small, Eve’s father, David Kelly, inspired her with a love for mathematics. To him, math was a puzzle to solve or a game to play. His curiosity was contagious. After earning a B.S. in Applied Mathematics from North Carolina State University, her passion for solving logical and mathematical problems led her to a computer-programming career. One very kind husband and three children later, she has focused her love for learning on homeschooling her own children with her first entering university this fall. She loves to encourage other homeschooling families on their journey—and especially to share practical ways to ignite a love for math in students that currently find it impossible or boring. In her mind, the study of math should not be just another worksheet to complete, but an exploration into patterns that can be understood, applied to other subjects, and even enjoyed. In her free time, Eve enjoys spending time doing whatever her family happens to be into at the moment.

name: Francie Black
title: Founder
bio:
Bio: Francie Black has a twenty year progression through technology product training, project management, software development management, and business development. As a former Sr. Management Consultant she consulted with Fortune 500 companies, as well as early stage tech startups. Francie, now an independent technology consultant, works with various companies doing project management and technology training. She has syndicated technology segments that air on TV stations across the country. You can also see her live each week on the ABC WLOS News Tech Time Segment. Check out Francie’s tech tip blog at ipadwisdom.com.

Francie has an undergraduate degree from UNC Charlotte and an MBA from Pepperdine University in Malibu, CA.

name: Gail Berzins
bio: Gail Berzins graduated from Miami University (Ohio) with a degree in education. She spent 13 years teaching Home Economics. She has a passion for helping people preserve their memories and tell their stories for future generations.

name: Gladys V Kober
bio:
Gladys Kober has a master's degree in Extragalactic Astrophysics. She currently works as a data analyst and image processor at GSFC/NASA. Her tasks at NASA include producing color composite images from the Hubble Space Telescope for press release.She also works as adjunct faculty at Montgomery College where she teaches Astronomy 101.

Master's degree in Astrophysics - She currently works as a data analyst in astronomy and image processor at GSFC/NASA. She also works as adjunct faculty at Montgomery College where she teaches Astronomy 101

name: Greg Baran
bio:
Hi, I’m Greg. I’m blessed every day because my wonderful wife Heather and my amazing son Nate are in my life. For years, I taught college and secondary English studies. From day one, it was apparent that most students struggled with writing. Due to my background in English Composition, I knew this problem was correctable, and it became my mission to make sure that every student who left my classroom could write clearly and effectively. That work started The Baran Method and it continues to drive me to help every child become a successful writer.
Greg has spent many years teaching English Composition and ELA at both the college and secondary levels. From his very first day in the classroom, he recognized how deeply many students struggled with writing. Drawing on his background in English Composition, Greg saw that this challenge was not only common but also correctable. Determined to make a difference, he made it his mission to ensure that every student who left his classroom could write with clarity and confidence. That commitment gave rise to The Baran Method, a practical approach to writing instruction that has helped thousands of children become successful writers.

name: Gretchen Roe
bio:
Gretchen H. Roe is a seasoned homeschooling advocate, educator, and speaker with over 21 years of experience homeschooling her six children, four of whom are now college graduates. Holding a degree in Psychology and Child Development, Gretchen has dedicated 19 years to supporting homeschooling families, with a particular focus on those with children who face learning challenges. Her empathetic and practical approach has made her a trusted voice in the homeschooling community.

Working with Demme Learning since 2014, Gretchen specializes in helping parents navigate math and language arts curricula, particularly for students who require tailored learning strategies. A former business owner and active member of various nonprofit boards, she brings a wealth of organizational and leadership experience to her presentations. Gretchen’s workshops are known for their actionable insights, warmth, and encouragement, empowering parents to foster confidence and success in their children’s education.

Beyond education, Gretchen is passionate about the outdoors, raising backyard chickens, beekeeping, and gardening. Her engaging and relatable style resonates with our attendees, offering practical tools and inspiration for homeschooling families.

name: Grey Cedars
bio:
Grey Cedars is a successful autistic adult who brings both lived experience and professional expertise to working with neurodivergent learners and their families. Grey is the Chief Creative Officer at The Learning Gardens with a bachelor’s degree in Molecular and Cellular Biology from Cedarville University.
