Grey is deeply committed to helping neurodivergent children—and their parents—understand one another more clearly. Grey excels at “translating” between internal experiences and outward behaviors, building bridges in communication so families can connect with greater empathy, clarity, and confidence. Grey brings hope, insight, and a grounded perspective on what is possible for neurodivergent individuals to achieve in education, relationships, and life.

name: Henderson Riley
bio:
Henderson Riley, affectionately known as “Mr. H” or “Mr. Chill” to his students, is an engaging and dynamic speaker, educator, and Florida Certified Math Teacher with over 20 years of experience in mathematics education. As the Director of Curriculum and Instruction for Mr. D Math, a leading online math education program, Henderson has spent the past decade inspiring students and parents with his innovative, hands-on teaching style. His ability to make complex math concepts accessible through humor, clarity, and enthusiasm has earned him a devoted following in the homeschooling community.

Mr. Riley’s presentations are celebrated for their interactive and practical approach, leaving attendees motivated and equipped with strategies to succeed in math education. His workshops focus on empowering homeschool parents and students by demystifying math, fostering a love for learning, and providing tools to build confidence in problem-solving. With a knack for connecting with audiences, Henderson’s sessions are both inspiring and transformative, making him an attendee favorite at our Great Homeschool Conventions.

Whether addressing students, parents, or educators, Henderson Riley brings a passion for math education, a wealth of experience, and a commitment to making learning engaging and impactful.

name: Jan Bloom
bio: Years and years ago, Jan and her husband, Gary, homeschooled their three children through high school, learning lots about great books along the way. Jan began buying and selling great books to other homeschoolers in Minnesota in 1989, and, after 10 years of booking, put together a book, WHO SHOULD WE THEN READ?, to help her customers get to know these good authors who wrote great books. In 2000, after their kids had graduated, Jan and Gary began traveling and selling books at homeschool conventions in other states. (Years and years and years ago Jan received her BS in Secondary Education, with a major in Physics and a minor in Math.)

name: Janine Deems
bio:
Janine Deems, CEO & Educational Strategist
I’m an Educational Strategist, the founder of Smarter By 1 Degree, Compass Virtual Academy, and a teacher of 24 years. I've spent my career helping students reach their educational goals, but I realized that time and money were barriers that kept many from attaining it. I love applying strategy to every situation, and when you apply it to education, it can allow you to get the education you want, at the price you can afford. We've all been told that there is one way to earn a degree, but the truth is that you can earn a degree around your schedule, for a fraction of the cost, and in a fraction of the time.
Smarter By 1 Degree started with me helping my daughter graduate high school with a bachelor’s degree at 16, debt-free. Fast forward 8 years, over ten thousand students, and a team of 16 amazing individuals who support our students every step of the way. We've grown from a simple vision to a thriving community of students reaching their full potential, achieving their dreams, and breaking through traditional barriers to success.
When I’m not helping students save time and money, I enjoy spending time with my husband, daughter, and my extended family. If you see me, chances are I'm laughing—because life is too short not to find humor in every situation

name: Jean Burk
bio:
Jean Burk is an acclaimed author, speaker, and homeschooling expert, renowned for her dynamic workshops that empower parents to guide their children toward college success. A former public-school teacher and homeschooling mother of two, Jean developed a passion for helping students excel academically, particularly in standardized test preparation. She is the creator of the award-winning College Prep Genius program, a comprehensive SAT, ACT, and PSAT/NMSQT preparation system that has helped thousands of students raise their test scores, secure scholarships, and gain admission to top-tier colleges.

Jean’s journey began when her own children earned full college scholarships through high test scores, inspiring her to share her strategies with other families. Her book, College Prep Genius: The No Brainer Way to SAT Success, along with her online and in-person courses, breaks down test-taking strategies into practical, easy-to-understand steps, making college preparation accessible and stress-free. Her expertise has been featured in media outlets like Home School Enrichment Magazine and Fox Business News.

As a speaker at Great Homeschool Conventions, Jean’s audiences love her energetic, encouraging, and practical presentations. Her workshops cover topics such as mastering standardized tests, securing college scholarships, and navigating the college admissions process. Known for her humor and relatable approach, Jean equips parents and students with tools to achieve academic and financial success without the overwhelm. A Texas-based homeschooling veteran, she continues to inspire families to unlock their potential through education.

name: Jeanna Young
bio:
Author of the Zondervan series, The Princess Parables, Jeanna Young homeschooled four grown children all the way through high school. Each has their own story, but all of them were college bound. She is on the last one, graduating in 2026! She has been married to a godly team partner, Bruce for 27 years.
After 20 years as an event planner, God called her to write Godly princess books full of character and purpose. After 14 years of successful sharing Jesus's parables with young girls, she was excited to branch into being a curriculum writer for Generous Families, Brave Books and Kendu Kids. Jeanna is also a walking medical miracle thanks to God, having been healed from cancer twice, having celebrated 10 years cancer free in 2025! She speaks to moms around the country rallying them to be the best moms they can be!

Jeanna Young is the co-author and creator of The Princess Parables.After 20 years as an event planner, God called her to write Godly princess books full of character and purpose. Recently, has found herself writing curriculum for Generous Families, Brave Books and now Kendu Kids. Her favorite hobby is speaking to moms around the country about parenting and living a great story.
Jeanna is mom to four wonderful children and homeschooled for 19 years. Two of her children are successful adulting (it can be done!), one attends Baylor University and one is a homeschooled junior in high school. She is wife to an amazing Godly man, Bruce for 25 years. Together they are privileged to be raising up the next generation of Godly men and women! Jeanna is also a walking medical miracle thanks to God, having been healed from cancer twice. When she is not writing, you can find her decorating, drinking coffee, or playing pickleball!

name: Jeannie Fulbright
bio:
Homeschool veteran of four top UGA scholarship graduates, Jeannie Fulbright has dedicated her life to the edification of homeschoolers. In addition to speaking, blogging, podcasting, writing articles, and personal ministry, Jeannie is the author of the #1 best-selling, multi-award-winning Apologia elementary science series, Living Streams American History, Culture & Craft Enrichment, as well as many products, curricula, and living books.

Jeannie grew up in Austin, Texas, and received her Bachelor of Arts from the University of Texas at Austin and Master of Fine Arts in Creative Writing from Fairfield University. She is also a Certified Christian Life Coach. She and her husband Jeff live in Johns Creek, Georgia. When Jeannie is not writing books, she’s doing crafts with her grandchildren, reading, or boating on Lake Lanier with her husband.

name: Jeannine Ramer
bio: Jeannine Ramer serves on the Leadership Team for Christian Home Educators of Ohio (CHEO) and has a deep heart for encouraging and equipping homeschooling families. With years of experience walking alongside parents through both the joys and challenges of homeschooling, Jeannine is passionate about helping families remain faithful to their God-given calling - especially during difficult or uncertain seasons.

name: Jeff Bennett
bio:
Former U.S. Air Force, homeschool dad, homeschool coop leader, Indiana Association of Homeschool Educators (IAHE), missionary, pastor, retail store manager, and Graduate of Liberty University, is putting his experience to work with the Be United In Christ Outreach Ministry to teach and promote Bible-based, love-based Christian unity in accordance with God's Word.

Director of Operations of our warehouse book distribution center for Be United In Christ Outreach Ministry. Teaching and promoting Bible-based, love-based Christian unity.

name: Jeff Reep
bio: Jeff is presently the Director of Career Services, Adjunct Faculty and CU LEADership Instructor at Cedarville University. He and his wife Criss homeschooled their 5 boys for 26 consecutive years, and they conduct marriage mentoring for college students. Jeff coached collegiate basketball in NCAA Division I, was twice named NCCAA Regional Coach of the Year, and has been recognized as the Staff Member of the Year. He is a Certified Professional Career Coach and brings a wealth of experience to Career Services and the classroom. Jeff is a gifted teacher with an ability to make the complex simple.

name: Jennifer Zajac
bio: Jennifer Zajac started working for TOPS Learning Systems in 2011, helping homeschooling families find solutions for their science curriculum needs, after realizing what a hidden gem TOPS was for her own homeschooling family. Let's find out how to create a customized, hands-on science curriculum that is not out of your budget and can be enriching for the whole family!

name: Jessica Parnell
title: CEO
bio:
Jessica Parnell, CEO of Edovate Learning Corp, is a passionate advocate for “learning without walls” and the author of the series Unlocking Your Child’s Genius. As the creator of Elephango.com and the visionary behind the Discover! K-8 curriculum, Jessica integrates her love for God’s design with her expertise in neuroscience, neurodivergence, and emotional intelligence. Her mission is to equip families with the tools they need to meet children where they are, fostering a sense of purpose and igniting a lifelong love for learning.

With over two decades of experience in education, Jessica has helped countless families turn challenges into opportunities, empowering students to embrace their unique gifts and thrive. As a board member of the Homeschool Trade Association, Jessica is deeply committed to supporting homeschool parents with innovative resources and strategies tailored to today’s educational needs. Her sessions inspire and equip parents to create transformative learning experiences that nurture their child’s potential and prepare them for a purposeful future.

name: Joel Salatin
bio:
Joel F. Salatin, born February 24, 1957, is a pioneering American farmer, lecturer, and author whose innovative, nature-inspired farming practices at Polyface Farms in Swoope, Virginia, have made him a global icon in sustainable agriculture and a leading figure in the modern homesteading movement. Self-described as a “Christian libertarian environmentalist capitalist lunatic farmer,” Salatin has transformed a worn-out 550-acre farm into a thriving, multi-generational operation that grosses over $2 million annually, supporting 20 full-time salaries and a robust internship program. His philosophy, rooted in mimicking natural ecosystems, emphasizes regenerative farming that heals the land while producing nutrient-dense food. His influence has been amplified through high-profile appearances on The Joe Rogan Experience (episodes #479 and #1478) and The Jordan B. Peterson Podcast (episode #539), as well as documentaries about his life and work, including The Lunatic Farmer by Angel Studios and films by Canon Press.

Raised in a family passionate about stewardship, Salatin’s parents purchased the eroded Shenandoah Valley farm in 1961 after losing a 1,000-acre farm in Venezuela to political upheaval. Influenced by his grandfather’s subscription to J.I. Rodale’s Organic Gardening and Farming Magazine, Salatin’s father rejected chemical-based agriculture, opting for compost and ecological methods. Joel began his own ventures in high school, selling rabbits, eggs, and poultry at the Staunton Curb Market. After earning a B.A. in English from Bob Jones University in 1979, where he met his wife, Teresa, Salatin briefly worked as a feature writer for The News Leader before returning to Polyface in 1982 to farm full-time with Teresa. Together, Joel and Teresa homeschooled their son, Daniel, instilling values of self-reliance and stewardship central to their homesteading lifestyle.

At Polyface, Salatin pioneered methods like rotational grazing, pastured poultry, and “mob stocking,” moving cattle, chickens, pigs, turkeys, and rabbits across pastures to mimic natural herbivore patterns. His farm, often called a “grass-farming” operation, uses no chemical fertilizers, pesticides, or vaccinations, relying instead on compost, mobile fencing, and natural animal behaviors to build soil fertility. Polyface produces salad bar beef, pigaerator pork, pastured poultry, eggs, and forestry products, serving over 5,000 families, 50 restaurants, and 10 retail outlets through direct marketing and a Metropolitan Buying Club. The farm’s on-site store and nationwide shipping program, launched in 2020, further extend its reach.

A prolific author of 16 books, including Pastured Poultry Profits (1993), You Can Farm (1998), and Folks, This Ain’t Normal (2011), Salatin is a sought-after speaker, delivering engaging lectures worldwide on regenerative farming, homesteading, homeschooling and food systems. Featured in Michael Pollan’s The Omnivore’s Dilemma, documentaries like Food, Inc., Fresh, The Lunatic Farmer (Angel Studios), he advocates for local, transparent food systems, rejecting government subsidies and organic certification in favor of decentralization and consumer-farmer connections. As editor of The Stockman Grass Farmer and a contributor to magazines like Plain Values, Salatin mentors aspiring farmers and homesteaders through Polyface’s workshops and apprenticeships.

name: Joelle Hodge
bio: Joelle Hodge holds a BA in history/political science from Messiah University in Grantham, Pennsylvania, and a Masters of Arts in Teaching from Eastern University’s Templeton Honors College. She began her career as a staffer to United States Senator Arlen Specter (R-Pa) before finding her professional home in the world of classical education in 1999. She has twenty years of classical education teaching experience. She has co-authored two logic books, The Art of Argument: An Introduction to the Informal Fallacies and The Discovery of Deduction: An Introduction to Formal Logic, both published by Classical Academic Press (CAP), and continues to support various editorial projects at CAP. Her primary focus is on supporting the growth and development of Classical Academic Press in her role as President.

name: Joey Mascio
bio:
Joey Mascio is a comedian, speaker, certified cognitive behavioral coach, and former middle school teacher. He helps teens build self-confidence, improve social skills, and become more productive. Before that, he worked as a Disneyland character performer and trained as a professional improvisational actor. Joey teaches tweens and teens how to build habits for success using humor, heart, stories, and inspiration. He created Sidekick to Hero, a gamified mindset training app for teens. He’s also a guest teacher in the Tuttle Twins Academy. Joey and his wife homeschool their four amazing kids.

Joey Mascio is a comedian, certified life coach, and former middle school teacher and counselor who helps teens with self-confidence, social skills, and goals. He was a Disneyland performer and is currently in the weekend cast at ComedySportz. During his seven years as a middle school teacher, he volunteered to be the teacher in the discipline office. His principal thought he was crazy (and she was right, but for different reasons). That’s where he started coaching teens on confidence, social skills, and goals. He started his own private coaching practice and left teaching to pursue it full time in 2021. He is the creator of a gamified confidence & resilience training app for teens, Sidekick to Hero, and host of the teen podcast, “Secrets for an Awesome Life.” He and his wife homeschool their four children.

name: John D. Mays
bio: After receiving his BS in Electrical Engineering from Texas A&M University, John D. Mays spent 14 years in industry in engineering and engineering management in the areas of electrical, controls and telecommunications systems. Vocationally drawn toward the field of education, John acquired a Master of Education degree in Secondary Education from the University of Houston in 1989, and subsequently completed 36 hours of graduate study in Physics at Texas A&M. Shortly after joining the faculty at Regents School of Austin in 1999, John began work on an Master of Liberal Arts at St. Edward's University, which he completed in 2003. John served as the Math-Science Department Chair at Regents School from 2001 until 2009 when he became Director of the Laser Optics Lab at Regents. He founded Novare Science & Math in 2009 and is the author of numerous student science texts and teacher resources. In 2019, Novare Science became part of Classical Academic Press, where John was Director of Science Curriculum overseeing continued development of the Novare Science curriculum. At the end of 2025, John entered semi-retirement as he winds up work on the last of his publications.

name: John De Gree
bio:
John De Gree is a veteran educator, historian, and founder of The Classical Historian, a company dedicated to teaching history through engaging curricula, games, and Socratic discussion. With over 26 years as a classroom teacher in public and private schools and 16 years as a homeschooling parent, John brings a wealth of experience to his mission of fostering critical thinking and historical understanding. Born and raised in Southern California as the tenth of eleven children, he credits lively family discussions with shaping his innovative approach to education.

John holds a B.A. from the University of Redlands, with studies in Washington, D.C., Vienna, Austria, and Schwabisch Hall, Germany, as well as Czech and German studies at Charles University in Prague. He earned a teaching credential from Humboldt State University and a Master’s of Arts in Liberal Arts with an emphasis in History from Cal State Dominguez Hills.

After marrying his wife, Zdenka, in the early 1990s, John lived in the Czech Republic and Turkey, where they founded a windsurfing school and taught history and English. Since 1996, they have resided in San Clemente, California, where they homeschooled five of their seven children. In 2011, John founded The Classical Historian, creating award-winning resources like World History Detective and The Story of Liberty: America’s Ancient Heritage Through the Civil War, which emphasize analytical skills, Socratic discussion, and the roots of American exceptionalism. His Take a Stand! series encourages students to think like historians, analyzing primary sources and debating historical perspectives.

As a favorite speaker at Great Homeschool Conventions, John is known for his passionate, practical, and inspiring presentations that equip parents and educators to teach history effectively. His work reflects a commitment to the Western educational tradition, emphasizing honesty, virtue, and logical analysis.

John and Zdenka, married for over 30 years, are proud parents and grandparents, and he remains an active educator, teaching middle school history at MacArthur Fundamental Intermediate School in Santa Ana, California.

name: John Futch
bio:
John Futch is the founder of Notelife, a friendly music studio based in Lexington, KY serving ages 8 and up. A musician for 40 years and music teacher for 25+, John focuses on simple, step-by-step instruction that uplifts and instills confidence. His online music curriculum combines bite-sized video lessons, lesson sheets and printouts, and optional live sessions (in-studio or Zoom). Families appreciate John’s practical practice strategies—like short daily drills that build momentum—along with his warm, encouraging teaching style. Whether a student is brand new or returning to music, the goal is steady progress and a joyful experience.

"John has been playing music for thirty-five years, and has taught music for over twenty years in his hometown of Lexington, KY. An accomplished musician, teacher, and author, in 2017 John co-founded First Frets, an online guitar and ukulele program that has now helped thousands of students of all ages. His forthcoming book, Music for Life, explores the many ways music enriches, heals, and helps us through every season of our lives.

name: John Lovell
bio:
John Lovell is the embodiment of the "Warrior Poet," blending strength, wisdom, and faith as the Founder and CEO of the Warrior Poet Society, a values-driven community dedicated to physical protection, truth-seeking, and living for a higher purpose. With over 100 million views across social media and streaming platforms, Lovell inspires millions to become protectors who are both dangerous and good—lovers and fighters, lions and lambs. His mission is to forge individuals who balance intellectual conviction with tactical expertise.

A former war veteran, Lovell served in the 2nd Ranger Battalion of the 75th Ranger Regiment, completing multiple combat tours in Iraq and Afghanistan. His military background shaped his expertise in firearms, tactics, and leadership, which he now shares as a credentialed NRA instructor and full-time firearms and tactics trainer. Teaching over 20 courses, including defensive pistol, rifle, night vision, low-light tactics, and home defense, Lovell emphasizes that training is critical for effective protection. Following his military service, he spent four years as a Christian missionary in Central America, reflecting his deep commitment to faith and service, which continues in his current role as a pastor.

In 2016, Lovell founded the Warrior Poet Society to counter the gun industry’s superficial bravado. Frustrated by portrayals of protectors as thoughtless aggressors, he built a community valuing both mind and body. The Warrior Poet Society now offers tactical gear, training courses, and a subscription-based network with instructional and philosophical content, including Lovell’s YouTube channel, boasting over 1.2 million subscribers. He is also a Wall Street Journal best-selling author of The Warrior Poet Way: A Guide to Living Free and Dying Well (2023), a practical and philosophical guide addressing the crisis of masculinity and promoting holistic manhood.

John Lovell is a dedicated family man, married to Rebekah Lovell, and a homeschooling dad to their two sons. Living on a small farm in Georgia, he embraces homesteading, balancing his roles as a husband, father, pastor, and educator. His content on The John Lovell Show often blends humor, including his signature "dad jokes," with discussions on family, faith, and personal growth. A public speaker with a B.A. in Business, Lovell is a lifelong student of war, philosophy, theology, and history, driven by a passion for learning and teaching.

Through the Warrior Poet Society, Lovell empowers police officers, military personnel, first responders, and civilians to protect others while living with purpose. His vision is a global community of warrior poets who challenge cultural narratives pitting strength against compassion, inspiring men and women to live free and die well with ferocity and refinement.

name: Josh Thomas
title: CEO
bio:
Josh Thomas is an educator and homesteader dedicated to helping families revive practical, traditional skills in a modern learning world. He has over 20 years of experience in homesteading, homeschooling, and family-centered skill development, and has taught thousands of parents and students through the School of Traditional Skills' online curriculum and workshops.

Through his work, Josh focuses on giving families practical, confidence-building instruction that introduces students to skills like animal husbandry, home-scale food production and preservation, home economics, and foundational shop skills. His teaching blends step-by-step demonstrations with real-life application so students gain competence, capability, and a sense of meaningful connection to their work — no farm, acreage, or prior experience required.

Josh, along with his team, develops classes, electives, and family resources specifically designed for homeschool and private-school parents who want their children to learn traditional skills alongside core academics. He believes these skills cultivate problem-solving, creativity, resilience, and hands-on confidence — qualities that serve students for life.

When not teaching, writing, or filming workshops, Josh lives with his wife Carolyn and their children on their 40-acre homestead in the mountains of North Idaho, where they garden, raise animals, and continue testing and refining the traditional methods they teach to families around the world.

name: Julia Nalle
title: Co-Owner
bio:
If you spend more than a moment with Julia Nalle, you’ll find that she’s passionate about Christ, her family, adoption and Classical/Charlotte Mason home education. Julia and her husband Rob live in a book-filled little house in the woods of central Virginia with their children. Julia has a B.S. in Early Childhood Education and a M.A. in Religion with emphasis in O.T. Studies, Counseling and Pastoral Ministries. Julia and her husband Rob write and produce BiblioPlan, a Classical history and literature curriculum with a Charlotte Mason flair. With numerous years of experience in classroom settings, homeschooling her own children, and tutoring children with special needs Julia has encountered it all. Julia has learned how to adapt, adjust and creatively engage children on a wide range of spectrums. In her sessions she loves to share her heart while giving numerous practical and easy ways to teach and inspire children.

.

name: Julie Levengood
bio:
Julie Levengood is the Group Tour Manager for the Tuscarawas County (Ohio) Convention and Visitors Bureau.
Julie has a passion for travel and enjoys working with tour and motorcoach operators as well as homeschool and niche groups to create memorable and exciting experiences in and around Tuscarawas County. She is also passionate about education and exploring the world around our lives.
Julie holds a master’s degree in public administration from Kent State University where she has also served as an adjunct professor in the Political Science department. She sits on the board of directors for the Dennison Railroad Depot Museum, RTY, Inc, Ohio Has It!, and the Virginia Motorcoach Association.
In her free time, Julie can often be found traveling, taking in a theater performance, or exploring new areas with her friends and family. Julie lives in New Philadelphia, Ohio with her three four-legged children: Farley, Butters, and Finn.

name: Kari Ann
bio:
Kari Ann, a self-taught, passionate, Christian music artist; young entrepreneur; and early high school graduate, stands with a platform of “Using Your Youth for The Lord.” At thirteen, she was a serial entrepreneur, launching and sustaining multiple annual community events placing emphasis on family centered activities.All the while, Kari has impressed many as a musical professional and has performed for four years in front of audiences in the hundreds. In 2022, she showcased her talent and took home the title of Miss Teen Wyoming.
Over the past two years and on-going, Kari has traveled across the nation as the primary spokesperson for Ready Lamp LLC, speaking to large-scale company officials and marketing her family’s game, “Civil Dispute.” She has connected with macro-influencers, honed skills in advertisement development, professional artwork production, web design, and mass communications. Most recently and with the much appreciated critiquing eyes and ears of her family, she wrote, self-produced, and released her first original song at only seventeen. Her song, “Isn’t Over Yet,” officially launched her music career and has been well received by listeners of all ages eager to purchase their own copy. She has caught the attention of those with connections to Nashville’s music industry and Disney.She currently is in the process of producing more songs, and hopes that God will use her talents and youth to reach the masses for His glory. Her other hobbies include crocheting, content creation, and family activities.

name: Karim Morato
bio:
Karim is originally from Guatemala and emigrated to the United States in 1992. Karim and her husband homeschooled their three children from preschool to college. She is an expert in foreign language learning, and is particularly focused on providing strategic advice and encouragement for Hispanic homeschooling families. 

Karim holds her master’s degree in curriculum and instruction, earned at George Mason University in Virginia. For more than 25 years, she has been teaching and serving the Hispanic community through educational advice, mentoring, and counseling. One of her passions is to equip parents to lead their children's education and advocate for what is best for them. She is convinced that homeschooling can bridge the academic gaps that sometimes exist in the Hispanic community.

Besides her homeschooling experience, Karim is a certified Spanish teacher with 16 years of teaching experience in public schools. In her work with HSLDA, Karim dedicates her time to upholding Hispanic leaders who support homeschooling and Hispanic parents who wish to educate at home. She aims to encourage parents to continue their homeschooling journey and enjoy their children. Beyond her position at HSLDA, Karim is passionate about cultures, multicultural work, and programs that advance higher education.

name: Kathyrn Gomes
bio:
Author, M.Ed., Speaker, Math Teacher Kathryn (Bell) Gomes, M.Ed. is the author of Apologia’s Exploring Creation with Mathematics series, a nationwide speaker, and a veteran math teacher. As a former homeschooler she is committed to equipping homeschool families to navigate the challenges of learning math with confidence. Kathryn holds degrees in mathematics from the University of Pittsburgh and the University of Pennsylvania. She has two decades of experience teaching math to homeschoolers, as well as a background in inner-city and multicultural classrooms.
Author, M.Ed., Speaker, Math Teacher Kathryn (Bell) Gomes, M.Ed. is the author of Apologia’s Exploring Creation with Mathematics series, a nationwide speaker, and a veteran math teacher. As a former homeschooler she is committed to equipping homeschool families to navigate the challenges of learning math with confidence. Kathryn holds degrees in mathematics from the University of Pittsburgh and the University of Pennsylvania. She has two decades of experience teaching math to homeschoolers, as well as a background in inner-city and multicultural classrooms. Known for

name: Kelly Love
bio:
Kelly's career journey began in his college days when he ambitiously built a thriving direct sales business, becoming an independent business owner.

Kelly then bought Wacky Fun Factory, a gumball machine manufacturer. Kelly doubled the company's sales in record time. Under Kelly's leadership, the company experienced exponential growth, expanding its reach with nine international offices across the world.

After selling Wacky Fun Factory, Kelly transitioned to a freelance role as an independent contractor for a Nashville-based online marketing firm. His remarkable success was recognized with two consecutive Rolex Executive Partner of the Year awards.

In 2011, Kelly's path led him to New York City, where he embraced the role of Executive Pastor at Gallery Church, contributing to the growth of a vibrant church plant in Manhattan.

By 2014, Kelly founded a natural-based skincare company, which rapidly grew to over 11,000 independent consultants within two years.

Today, Kelly has returned to his first passion—ministry. As the National Sales and Events Director with Trail Life USA, he is dedicated to making a meaningful impact by engaging with the masculine hearts of America, addressing one of the most pressing issues of our time.

https://www.traillifeusa.com/

name: Kerri Shepard
bio: Kerri Shepard is a teacher, speaker, reconciler, author, and mentor with over 25 years' experience in ministry. She developed Promise Keys children's curriculum out of her passion that children would know God intimately and experientially for themselves--that they would know the Person of God, not just the system of God. She is a licensed and ordained minister and lives in the Fort Worth area with her husband, Jon. Kerri and Jon have two young adult sons who still remember their Promise Keys today.

name: Kevin Grasso
bio: Kevin Grasso (PhD, Hebrew University of Jerusalem) is a linguist and Bible scholar who founded Biblingo, a software company to teach anyone and everyone Biblical Greek and Hebrew. He lived and studied in Israel for five years and participates regularly in scholarly discourse on the Bible. He is also a homeschool dad of 5 and shares teaching duties with his wife, Rachel. The Grasso family live just outside of Atlanta, GA.

name: Kevin Krebs
bio:
Kevin Krebs is the founder, managing director, and heartbeat of HelloCollege. Inspired by his parents, Kevin spent the last 25 years helping families successfully navigate college admissions, leveraging his education from Northwestern University and Emmy-winning career as a broadcast journalist to mentor students in building standout college applications that highlight each individual’s advantages.
Kevin founded HelloCollege with the goal of helping students and parents navigate the college path while raising awareness about the escalating costs and increasingly competitive nature of higher education. Kevin and his team are on a mission to guide the next generation of students to gain admission into their best fit colleges, ensure they graduate within four years without overwhelming debt, and equip them with a well-defined career plan to land their dream job.
Throughout his career, Kevin has shared his knowledge and expertise, delivering over 500 hundred college planning and career readiness workshops, providing thousands of consultations, and helping over 40,000 families across the US and internationally gain admission to college.

name: Kiri Jorgensen
bio:
Kiri Jorgensen is the Publisher and Senior Editor at Chicken Scratch Books. She is also a writer, teacher, entrepreneur, and most importantly wife and mother. Her involvement in the world of children’s literature spans almost three decades with perspective from many angles—writer, editor, organization leader, and writing instructor for both kids and adults. She also taught school for 16 years where she specialized in gifted and rural education. She loves all books middle grade, but in her heart, she is a teacher. Chicken Scratch Books lets her combine all her passions. Besides the world of teaching and story, Kiri loves gardening, sewing quilts, being in nature, and bragging about her four amazing kids. She lives with her husband and a plethora of animals in rural Montana, where she cooks her meals on a wood-burning cookstove and is grateful every day for being right where she is.

Kiri Jorgensen is the Publisher and Senior Editor at Chicken Scratch Books. Her involvement in the world of children’s literature spans three decades and includes teaching, writing, editing, and leadership. Her passion is all books middle grade, and then teaching kids how to embrace those books too. Kiri loves bragging about her four amazing kids, and lives with her husband and a plethora of animals in rural Montana, where she cooks her meals on a wood-burning cookstove and is grateful every day for being right where she is.

name: Laura Islas/ Olivia Islas
bio:
We love homeschooling and love to inspire and encourage the next generation of homeschool families. We started homeschooling back in 1991 and homeschooled for 28 years with three grads. Two have degrees from Harvard and one is still in college (currently on his third NASA internship). We are here to tell you that even as a single mom, and experiencing many challenges, by hanging on to our Lord’s hand, He will not only lead you through but give you joy! Homeschooling is an amazing opportunity to connect as a family, grow in your faith, and have so much fun in the process! Learn some classic and stress free ways of getting the most out of your homeschooling journey.

The Adventure Club at North Winds Institute is designed to bring parents, kids, and anyone together in a shared pursuit of adventure, resilience, and unconventional living. The club fosters a lifestyle that values curiosity, grit, and growth through hands-on experiences, calculated risks, and a deep connection to the natural world and each other. Whether scaling a mountain, navigating a river, or uncovering hidden histories, or building a rocket, families build character and bonds that last a lifetime.

name: Laurie Christine
bio:
Laurie Christine is an author, podcast host, speaker, Bible teacher, wife, and mom of four wild, loud, adventurous boys. Laurie is the author of The Dragon Slayer Bible Series, adventure-packed biblical fiction and devotions for kids ages 8-12.
Her podcast, The Sword and Story Podcast, helps Christian parents discover exciting, faith-filled books for their boys.
Laurie’s passion is to raise strong, courageous warriors for the Kingdom of God, and to help families like yours do the same.

name: Leia LeMaster Horton
bio: Leia LeMaster Horton, M.Ed. is the founder of Horton Test Prep and a nationally recognized Test Prep Professional and Scholarship Expert in the college prep industry. She regularly speaks at conferences and college fairs across the country, including UC Berkeley, UT Austin, Clemson, Johns Hopkins and Columbia University. She has also been featured on numerous podcasts and webinars, guiding families through the often stressful college preparation process. As a founding member of the National Test Prep Association, a scholarship judge and a certified test prep instructor, Leia is passionate about helping students raise their ACT, CLT and SAT scores, unlock scholarship opportunities and make their dream schools a reality.

name: Lisa Jaeger
bio:
Lisa Jaeger – Homeschool Representative, eat2explore
With over 20 years of experience in education and 13 years as a dedicated homeschool mom, Lisa Jaeger brings both expertise and heart to every learning environment she touches. She holds a Master’s degree in Education with postgraduate work in Language and Literacy, and has spent her career helping students (and their parents) find joy and confidence in learning.
As a mom to a college sophomore and a high school senior, Lisa has navigated every stage of the homeschool journey — from early phonics to college applications. Beyond her own home classroom, she has served her homeschool community in countless roles, including volunteer teacher, field trip coordinator, and co-op president. Most recently, she founded an online co-op for high schoolers, creating new opportunities for students to connect and grow.
In her current role as the Homeschool Representative for eat2explore, Lisa combines her passion for education, culture, and hands-on learning to help families “unbox the world” — bringing geography, global cuisine, and adventure right to the kitchen table.

name: Lisa Nehring
bio:
Lisa Nehring has one husband, two graduate degrees, five kids, and a black belt in homeschooling. A 30-year homeschool veteran, she’s the powerhouse behind It’s Not That Hard to Homeschool and True North Academy. Known for her wit, wisdom, and no-nonsense encouragement, Lisa helps parents homeschool with clarity, confidence, and joy—while preparing their teens for college, career, and life.

Ready to homeschool like a pro? Laugh, learn, and leave inspired to chart your course True North.

 Website: https://truenorthhomeschool.academy/

Facebook Page: True North Homeschool Academy
Facebook Group: True North Homeschool Tribe
Pinterest: True North Homeschool Academy
Instagram @Truenorthhomeschoolacademy

name: Martin Cothran
title: Editor & Author
bio:
Martin Cothran (Memoria Press) is a brilliant writer, educator, and advocate for classical education, known for his significant contributions to the homeschooling movement and his leadership in promoting traditional learning methods. Residing in Danville, Kentucky, with his wife of over 32 years, Cothran is a father of four homeschooled adult children and a grandfather of two. As a co-founder of Memoria Press and Highlands Latin School in Louisville, Kentucky, he has played a pivotal role in advancing classical Christian education. He serves as the provost of Memoria College, director of the Classical Latin School Association, and editor of The Classical Teacher, a quarterly magazine for parents and educators published by Memoria Press.

Cothran’s academic background includes a B.A. in philosophy and economics from the University of California, Santa Barbara, and an M.A. in Christian Apologetics from the Simon Greenleaf School (now part of Trinity University). A former instructor of Latin, logic, and rhetoric at Highlands Latin School, he has authored several widely used textbooks for private and homeschool settings, including Traditional Logic: Books I and II, Material Logic: A Course on How to Think, Classical Rhetoric: A Study of Aristotle’s Principles of Persuasion, and Lingua Biblica: Old Testament Stories in Latin. These works, designed to be accessible yet rigorous, introduce students to formal logic, rhetoric, and classical languages, emphasizing critical thinking and the Western intellectual tradition.

As a leading voice in classical education, Cothran is a Featured Speaker the five, regional Great Homeschool Conventions where he addresses topics like literature, logic, and the value of a classical Christian education. His workshops, such as interactive sessions on analyzing great stories, inspire educators and parents to foster a love for learning. His writings appear in outlets like The Imaginative Conservative, Crisis Magazine, and major newspapers, including the Cincinnati Enquirer and Louisville Courier-Journal, where he explores themes of culture, literature, and Western civilization’s roots in Athens, Rome, and Jerusalem.

Beyond education, Cothran is a Senior Policy Analyst and registered lobbyist for The Family Foundation of Kentucky, where he has served for over 12 years, influencing state education policy and appearing frequently on Kentucky Educational Television’s Kentucky Tonight. His commentary on education and public policy is widely quoted in national outlets like Reuters, USA Today, The New York Times, and The Washington Post. A devout Christian, Cothran integrates his faith into his work, advocating for the preservation of Western cultural heritage and the importance of great books, as seen in his essays praising authors like G.K. Chesterton, his favorite, and defending the King James Bible.

Cothran’s passion for books extends to his personal life, where he cherishes their tactile and intellectual value, marking his copies with notes for his children. His vision is to equip students and families with the tools to think critically and live virtuously, making him a cornerstone of the classical homeschooling community.

name: Mary Morgan
bio: Mary calls Lansing, MI, home but has a passion to travel across America, exploring natural wonders in our national parks. She has been to all fifty states, visited sixty-two national parks, and has written ten books about her favorites. Now that she has two grandchildren, taking them on mystery trips to a national park is the perfect vacation. Her workshop will entertain and educate you on the highlights of many parks.

name: Massey Campos
title: Founder
bio:
Massey Campos is a dynamic pastor, national speaker, and founder of Self-Evident Ministries, a platform dedicated to educating Americans about the biblical heritage of the United States and promoting the gospel of Jesus Christ. A first-generation American born to Mexican immigrant parents, Campos grew up traveling with his family as his father served as an evangelist. This upbringing instilled in him a passion for preaching, which he began in his teens, sharing the message of Christ with congregations. Since 2001, he has spoken at high schools, colleges, churches, and civic groups across the country, advocating for the moral and biblical foundations of liberty and the U.S. Constitution. He is a much-loved Featured Speaker at our Great Homeschool Conventions, where he inspires homeschooling families with his energetic and engaging style, emphasizing faith, family, and freedom.

Campos serves as Associate Pastor at Revive Church in Stuart, Florida, a non-denominational church focused on uniting the Word and Spirit of God to foster encounters with Christ. With seven years of pastoral experience, including four as an Associate Pastor, he oversees a staff of 19, manages ministry growth plans, and preaches regularly during Sunday and Wednesday services. As President of Self-Evident Ministries, founded in 2015 with his wife, Cari, Campos leads an educational outreach that connects constitutional rights to faith, encouraging citizens to become productive members of society through workshops and unique curricula.

A homeschooling father of three boys, Campos, alongside Cari, who assists with his ministry and speaking engagements, is deeply committed to raising his children with a strong biblical worldview. His homeschooling philosophy aligns with his broader mission to restore America’s moral foundation, emphasizing the importance of educating the next generation in faith and constitutional principles. His speaking style, described as powerful and engaging, resonates with audiences of all ages, blending humor, passion, and deep knowledge of scripture and American history.

Originally from Sleepy Eye, Minnesota, where he attended Sleepy Eye Secondary School, Campos now resides in Port St. Lucie, Florida, with his family. His work with Self-Evident Ministries includes traveling nationwide to speak at churches, community events, and schools, aiming to inspire hope and action through faith and understanding of America’s founding documents. His social media presence, including platforms like Facebook and SoundCloud (@masseycampos, @selfevidentpod_), amplifies his message of liberty, morality, and the gospel. Campos’s mission is to see Americans embrace their God-given rights and responsibilities, fostering a nation grounded in biblical truth and constitutional integrity.

name: Matthew McKenzie
bio:
Matthew McKenzie grew up in the St. Louis metro area of Southern Illinois, but now resides with his wife, Amy, in the Winston-Salem area of North Carolina. They homeschooled all three of their children from K-12, and all three are now college graduates working full time in their chosen vocations.
Matthew has spent more than 30 years as an analyst in the insurance industry, during which time he has been actively serving in various church ministries. He has taught multiple adult classes focused on topics such as apologetics and the historicity of the Old Testament, as well as presenting at previous Great Homeschool Convention events. He and Amy have also been involved in local homeschool organizations.
Based on the experiences of their own family, Matthew and Amy share their recommendations of wholesome materials for parents who are interested in making good use of available recorded media from the 20th Century to both educate and entertain their families.

Matthew McKenzie grew up in the St. Louis metro area of Southern Illinois, but now resides with his wife, Amy, in the Winston-Salem area of North Carolina. They homeschooled all three of their children from K-12, and all three are now college graduates working full time in their chosen fields of employment. Matthew has spent more than 30 years as an analyst in the insurance industry, during which time he has been actively serving in various church ministries. He has taught multiple adult classes focused on topics such as apologetics and the historicity of the Old Testament. He and Amy have also been involved in local homeschool organizations. Early in life, Matthew developed an affinity for Golden Age radio shows (1930s through 1950s) and the vintage television programs of the 1950s and ‘60s. Simply by being routinely exposed to such media, his children also developed their own preferences for programming from that era and an enhanced understanding of the life and times of 20th Century Americans. Based on the experiences of their own family, Matthew and Amy share their recommendations of wholesome materials for parents who are interested in making good use of available recorded media from the 20th Century to both educate and entertain their families.

name: Melanie Elsey
bio: Melanie Elsey has been serving as the Legislative Liaison for Christian Home Educators of Ohio (CHEO) since 1994. In this capacity she has monitored the work of the State Board of Education, the Ohio Legislature, and Ohio Department of Education and Workforce to ensure state policies preserve and support the rights of parents to home educate their children. She and her husband John also serve as members of CHEO’s Leadership Team. She has publicly addressed the effects of state and federal policies at the invitation of more than 350 local communities since 1993. She and John have been married 43 years and are the parents of 3 married children and have 6 grandchildren.

name: Michelle Dunham, PhD
bio:
Michelle Dunham is a devoted mom of bookworms who loves reading aloud. Michelle is a former teacher who holds a masters degree in Human Learning and a doctorate in Educational Psychology and Research. Her passion for family reading and learning led her to found Ruby Reads, where she spends her time designing products that help families fall in love with reading together.

Michelle Dunham is a devoted mom of bookworms who loves reading aloud. Michelle is a former teacher who holds a masters degree in Human Learning and a doctorate in Educational Psychology and Research. Her passion for family reading and learning led her to found Ruby Reads, where she spends her time designing products that help families fall in love with reading together.

name: Michelle Peters
bio: Michelle Peters is the founder and teacher of Mrs. Peters' Math. Michelle originates from Indiana and has taught for over 20 years in both public schools and homeschool settings. First teaching in in both public middle and high schools, followed by homeschooling her then 4th grader, followed by teaching at her local homeschool co-op for the last 7 years, she has taken her expertise to devise her own online classes geared for the homeschooler. Michelle lives in Indianapolis, IN with her family. When not teaching, she enjoys family get togethers, traveling, paper crafting, and logic puzzles

name: Millie Florence
bio:
Homeschool graduate, Millie Florence’s earliest memories are of lying under the covers at night, whispering stories to herself long after her parents told her to go to sleep. She published her first book, Honey Butter, at age 13 and hasn’t stopped since! She’s gone on to publish The Balter of Ashton Harper (Bandersnatch Books), The Mage Pocket: Apprentice Alliance (Sprouting Pen Press), and Beyond Mulberry Glen (Waxwing Books), winner of the 2025 IPPY Gold Award in Juvenile Fiction.
Millie lives in a picturesque blue house in the woods of southern Illinois. She loves adventure, good food, and just about all things yellow. Learn more on her website, millieflorence.com

Homeschool graduate, Millie Florence’s earliest memories are of lying under the covers at night, whispering stories to herself long after her parents told her to go to sleep. She published her first book, Honey Butter, at age 13 and hasn’t stopped since! She’s gone on to publish The Balter of Ashton Harper and her newest book Beyond Mulberry Glen, released this year through Sarah Mackenzie’s publishing house Waxwing Books.
Millie lives in a picturesque blue house in the woods of southern Illinois. She loves adventure, good food, and just about all things yellow. Learn more on her website, millieflorence.com

name: Monica Irvine
bio:
Monica Irvine is the creator and President of The Etiquette Factory. As the published author of 5 books along with the storybook series, Fundamentals4Kids, Monica is a crowd favorite due to her wealth of knowledge on purposeful parenting. Monica leaves every crowd wanting more and more importantly, excited to leave and implement the strategies she teaches immediately into their Homelife. We are excited to announce that Monica is returning as featured speaker at Great Homeschool Convention.

https://www.theetiquettefactory.com

name: Natasha Cudd
bio: Natasha Cudd is the co-founder of From One Child to Another, an organization that seeks to empower K-12 students to use their God-given gifts to help those less fortunate and to teach students about the needs of the world. When Natasha was eight years old, she felt God’s call to raise funds for a school in Uganda, Africa. After hosting a read-a-thon and speaking at churches, schools, and conferences, the funds were raised over a period of five months. Since then, From One Child to Another has equipped many other students to discover their God-given talents that can be utilized to make a difference around the world. Natasha has since travelled to many countries across the Americas, Asia, and Africa working with major nonprofits, has completed an M.A. in Global Studies from Liberty University, and is currently pursuing a seminary degree. She has also packed thousands of shoeboxes for Samaritan’s Purse and Operation Christmas Child, developed missions curriculum for students of all ages, and spoken at multiple conferences and events. Natasha is also a recipient of the K-Love and Air1 Community Impact Award and American Fundraising Professional’s Outstanding Youth in Philanthropy Award, and she is a homeschool graduate who has spent over six years teaching and coaching homeschool students. Natasha is excited to share some practical strategies for engaging your students in the Great Commission.

name: Nell Branum
bio:
Nell Branum is the award-winning author of the Heroes on the Home Front Mysteries series (Moody Publishers) for middle-grade readers ages 8-12, as well as historical non-fiction picture books that she co-wrote with her parents. She is a former magazine editor and children’s librarian, and has been a published writer of missions-centered curriculum for preschoolers and children for more than 45 years.
As an educator, Nell knows the value of literature-based learning. As a devoted Christ-follower, she has a heart for helping kids understand that not only is it okay to be a Christian, it is actually smart. With a degree in Elementary and Early Childhood Education, a Master’s in Library and Information Services, a seminary graduate degree in Religious Education, and decades of telling stories to children, both inside her home and outside it, Nell knows that stories are often magnets that draw children (and all of us), helping us discover and internalize truths, relevance, faith, role models, and more.
Having found herself in a new chapter after the death of her minister husband, Nell now writes for children from her small-town home in Alabama. She keeps chickens in the back yard, enjoys blueberry-flavored coffee whenever possible, and is always ready for an adventure with her grown kids and nine grandchildren, including but not limited to international trips and helping each of her grands to write and publish a book during their third grade year on whatever topic they would like.

name: Nicholas Ellis
bio: Christian Halls International

name: Nicholeen Peck
title: President
bio:
When you see a completely packed workshop room at Great Homeschool Conventions, it could be because any one of our incredible Featured Speakers is scheduled for that session, but --- there’s always a decent chance that it’s because Nicholeen is speaking!

  • One attendee told us that she and her husband had flown in to attend Great Homeschool Conventions, and --- getting to hear Nicholeen Peck speak just one-time had made their trip completely worth it!

Nicholeen Peck is a globally recognized parenting expert, homeschooling advocate, author, and speaker whose proven system for teaching self-government has transformed countless families. Based in Tooele County, Utah, for over 27 years, Nicholeen, alongside her husband, Spencer, has raised four children—three of whom have graduated with university degrees, while also fostering numerous troubled teens. Her unique approach, rooted in calmness, communication, and four simple skills, brings order to chaotic homes and fosters self-discipline in children. As the founder of Teaching Self-Government and President of the Worldwide Organization for Women, an approved United Nations consultant, Nicholeen balances international influence with her role as a devoted stay-at-home mother and homeschooling parent.

Nicholeen’s journey into homeschooling was unexpected. Raised by a public-school teacher and college professor father and a patient, teaching mother, she excelled in traditional schooling and initially had no plans to homeschool. However, her desire for family unity and personalized education led her to homeschool some of her children, while others attended public school, tailoring their paths to individual needs. Her homeschooling philosophy emphasizes family unity, moral discussions, and self-motivated learning through classical literature, biographies, and project-based activities. Nicholeen’s approach, detailed in resources like her Home School Helper Series, focuses on creating a joyful learning environment through strong family communication and structured routines, avoiding over-reliance on textbooks. She advocates for parents to model lifelong learning to inspire their children, a principle she shares at global homeschooling conferences.

In 2009, Nicholeen gained international attention when her family was featured on the BBC’s The World’s Strictest Parents, where two troubled British teens stayed in their home for eight days. Within days, the teens transformed, embracing the Pecks’ self-government principles, showcasing Nicholeen’s ability to change hearts and behaviors. A former foster parent to teens with challenges like ADHD, OCD, and anger issues, she emphasizes teaching behaviors over relying on medication. Her best-selling book, Parenting: A House United (2008), translated into Spanish, Chinese, and Japanese, outlines her practical, principle-based parenting system, with additional titles like Roles and Popular Parenting Methods reinforcing her teachings.

A immensely popular speaker at events like Great Homeschool Conventions, Nicholeen delivers workshops on parenting, homeschooling, and self-government, addressing topics like obedience as the foundation of education and revitalizing homeschool environments. Her work extends to radio, news shows, and platforms like the Homeschool Highschool Podcast. As a Utah legislator (R-Tooele), she has advocated for homeschooling families, notably sponsoring HB209 in 2025 to remove an ineffective affidavit requirement for homeschool parents. Her mission is to heal families from emotional bondage through self-government, leaving a legacy of empowered, principled children.

name: Nick Freitas
bio:
Nick Freitas is a committed Christian, husband, father, former Green Beret, homesteader, homeschooler and influential conservative voice. Serving as a Virginia House of Delegates member (2016–2025) for District 62, Nick championed individual liberty, personal responsibility, and limited government. Born in Chico, California, on August 29, 1979, he served in the U.S. Army with the 82nd Airborne and 25th Light Infantry Divisions before becoming a Special Forces (Green Beret) Weapons and Intelligence Sergeant, completing two combat tours in Iraq and earning an honorable discharge in 2009.

Nick and his wife, Tina (his high school sweetheart), homeschooled their three children—Lillyana, Luke, and Alexandria. Their homeschooling journey, initially a more practical necessity due to Nick’s military service, became a commitment to fostering independent thinking and strong values. As an advocate for education freedom, Nick inspires homeschooling families with his emphasis on parental choice and raising principled citizens.

A dynamic communicator, Nick hosts Making the Argument with Nick Freitas, a podcast diving into political philosophy, current issues, and his military and legislative experiences. His YouTube channel, under his name, has over 1.2 million subscribers, with millions of views on his viral speeches and content promoting conservative principles, free markets, and cultural topics like the importance family and masculinity. His overall social media following exceeds 3.3 million, averaging 2 million daily views, resonating with audiences seeking a trusted and principled voice of common sense, clarity and conviction.

As a featured speaker at the 2026 Great Homeschool Conventions in South Carolina, Ohio, California, and Texas, Nick will inspire attendees from 38 states with insights on faith, family, education, and liberty. He will also join a special joint session for dads on Saturday morning in South Carolina, Ohio and Texas with John Lovell of the Warrior Poet Society. Nick’s blend of military discipline, legislative experience, and homeschooling expertise have made him a trusted and compelling voice for families navigating modern education and culture.

name: Olivia McCants
bio: Olivia McCants is a proud K-12 homeschool grad and chronic history nerd. As a child, she benefited from wonderful museum programming and now works to share similar opportunities and resources with students and families. Through the Kentucky Historical Society, she has developed regular “Homeschool History Day” events, drawing hundreds of students from Kentucky and the surrounding Ohio River Region. Olivia grew up attending GHC and is thrilled to offer something back to the homeschooling community.

name: Paola Brown
bio:
Paola Brown is an engaging speaker, author, and homeschooling mother of three, renowned for her innovative work in health education and homeopathy advocacy. As the founder and CEO of Paola Brown LLC and president of Americans for Homeopathy Choice, she empowers families to embrace holistic health through her workshops, online courses, and groundbreaking homeschool curricula. With a B.A. in English Education and an M.A. in Curriculum and Instruction from Arizona State University, both earned summa cum laude, Paola taught high school and college English for over a decade before transitioning to homeschooling her children in 2014.

Her signature project, Teach Me Health & Homeopathy, is the world’s first homeopathy curriculum for grades 1–12, complemented by Evie and the Golden Homestead and a Pre-K/Kindergarten Primer. These resources, along with her Homeopathy Heroes card game, blend storytelling and critical thinking to teach children about natural health. As a speaker at Great Homeschool Conventions, Paola’s dynamic presentations inspire parents with practical strategies to integrate homeopathic principles into family life, helping them “ditch Big Pharma” and foster true health. She has also led congressional briefings in Washington, D.C., advocating for the right to choose homeopathy.

Fluent in Portuguese and Spanish, Paola’s global perspective is enriched by her experience as a health instructor with Care for Life in Mozambique. Based in Texas, she and her family live on their “Milk and Honeybee Homestead,” practicing organic farming and homeopathy to sustain their health and livelihood. Her workshops are celebrated for their warmth, accessibility, and transformative impact, making her a beloved figure in the homeschooling and holistic health communities.

name: Rachel S. Donahue & Carolyn Clare Givens
bio:
Rachel Donahue isn’t a typical homeschooling mother of four: after finishing her English degree, she and her husband, Mick, moved to Spain and spent the next dozen years in cross-cultural ministry. When family circumstances brought them back to North Carolina, a return to literature ushered her into a new season of writing: both poetry and the beginnings of a novel. Friendships with other writers, combined with her editing experience and design work, led to the publication of her first book: Real Poems for Real Moms: from a Mother in the Trenches to Another. Rachel is the author of Beyond Chittering Cottage: Poems of Place, published by Bandersnatch Books in 2021.

Carolyn Clare Givens is a displaced Northerner exploring the foreign ways of the South. She works in communications at New City Church in North Carolina, handling much of the stuff that goes on screens or paper (or signs, or t-shirts, or pens, or mugs….). Carrie lives in Charlotte with her literary cat, Lord Peter Wimsey. She has previously bumped around the world, both as a missionary kid and adult. Carrie was part of the publishing process on more than 30 books before launching Bandersnatch. She’s the author of Rosefire, published by Bandersnatch Books in 2021, and The King’s Messenger, released as a new edition by Bandersnatch in 2022.

name: Rebekah Lovell
bio:
You don't want to miss hearing Rebekah Lovell this year at your regional GHC!  
Rebekah Lovell is an author, speaker, and Homeschool momma of two wild and noble boys. She has been married to John for 18 years, and they live on a homestead in northwest GA. Homeschooling for a decade, she’s passionate about the power of family culture and helping boys thrive by giving them what they need and understanding their wiring: noble purpose and adventure. Her first book, Boyhood Resurrected: Igniting a Revival of Daring and Heroic Boys comes out this fall and is available at Rebekahlovell.com or on Amazon.

Rebekah is also the founder of The Outdoor Reading Club, an initiative to get kids reading and spending more time outside, helping children and families spend memorable time together outside and in great books.

Her background includes a B.S. in Public Relations and work in Marketing, PR and sales before becoming a missionary in Central America, where she and John worked with a student program and church plant. Since then, she’s come alongside John in Warrior Poet Society videos on parenting, marriage, personal security, and home defense. Now, the Lovells serve in a local church plant in Rome, Georgia; Grace City Rome. Follow Rebekah on IG: @MrsPoet_Actual

Here are some early reviews on Rebekah's new book:

"As the leader of a national ministry focused on guiding boys into godly manhood, I know firsthand that boys aren’t defective girls—they are uniquely designed by God for boldness, purpose, and strength. Boyhood Resurrected captures this truth with clarity and conviction.
Rebekah Lovell calls us to remember what culture has forgotten: that boys thrive on adventure, challenge, and meaning. This book is a much-needed rallying cry to honor and fight for the hearts of our sons.
I stand with Rebekah—as a fellow fan of boys and a fellow warrior in their corner."
- Mark Hancock, CEO, Trail Life USA

"It’s no secret that boys today face an uphill battle. Rather than helping them channel their masculinity in constructive ways, our culture tells them that masculinity itself is toxic. That’s why I’m thankful that Rebekah Lovell has written this indispensable resource for parents who are looking for ways to help their sons flourish and embrace their God-given design."
 - Jim Daly, President – Focus on the Family

“I am deeply concerned about our boys. Young men are growing up in a culture that too-often rejects their gifts, labels them as incurably toxic, and regularly treats them as defective girls. Rebekah Lovell is a strong voice speaking out for the multitude of heroic mothers who have had enough of their sons being stereotyped, vilified, and sidelined. She has a sometimes controversial, often counter-cultural take on a positive vision for us to make a way for our sons to thrive and flourish as boys. We need more moms like her, quietly blessing her own boys and publicly advocating for all our sons.”
- S. D. Smith, author of The Found Boys and The Green Ember Series

"If you have boys, know boys, care about boys, please read this fabulous book! You will love it. And give a copy to everyone you know raising boys in this chaotic cultural moment!"
- John Eldrege, Founder of Wild at Heart Ministries

name: Regan Barr
bio: Regan Barr is father to 3 successfully homeschooled children, a Classical archaeologist who’s excavated in Greece, Turkey, and Jordan, a popular homeschool blogger, and a co-founder of The Lukeion Project. He has developed the most extensive Greek program available to high-school students online, offering 5 years of reading courses after the first 2 years of grammar. He specializes in ancient civilizations and Classical Greek, with an emphasis on Greek history, philosophy, and archaeology.

name: Rene Christensen
bio: Rene Christensen is an author, speaker, podcaster, homeschooling mom of seven, and founder of Training Them Wisely. Passionate about family discipleship, she equips parents to foster biblical literacy and worldview.

name: Rhonda Cregor
bio: Associate Director of New Student Relations; Christian wife (of 40years!) and mom of two graduates of ORU

name: Rob Currie, Ph.D.
bio: God has blessed me with many opportunities to speak to parents, including over sixty presentations to MOPS groups, three times at the Hearts at Home Convention, and something like ten workshops at Great Homeschool Conventions. I have decades of experience in public speaking as a university professor of psychology, but I know that the needs of a parenting are different from those of college students. Parents want solid information but they want it to be practical, practical, practical.

name: Ryan Hobbs
bio: Ryan, the director of MathHouseOnline.com, has been a college math lecturer for ten years and is the author of the Learn By Doing math curriculum series. He specializes in making math an active learning experience and in helping math strugglers succeed. Ryan is also the author of the Bible Club Mystery Series for middle school readers, which incorporates real Bible study into each mystery.

name: Ryan Lauterio
bio:
Ryan is a Christian conservative, multi-award-winning studio artist and educator whose work has been recognized with a Certificate of Special Congressional Recognition for his induction into the Palmdale High School Hall of Fame. Ryan’s professional journey weaves through the dynamic realms of painting, drawing, studio methods, curating, and deeply exploring the intersection of art and theology with over 30 years of experience.
Ryan boasts a substantial academic foundation, earning both a B.A. and an M.A. in Studio Art from CSU Sacramento, followed by an MFA in Painting and Printmaking from Virginia Commonwealth University (VCU). Complementing his extensive art education, Ryan’s theological understanding has been profoundly shaped by three years of rigorous in-house pastoral training at Remnant Church. He served as the Pastor for Art and Culture there from 2014 to 2020, a role that significantly enhanced his ability to weave his faith seamlessly into his artistic pursuits. Ryan is an Associate for Christian Art and Aesthetics at the Cantaro Institute in Niagara Canada.
Ryan, a former assistant professor for 17 years at Virginia Commonwealth University (VCU), which is recognized as the number one ranked public art school in the US, in the Art and Design Foundations program, has made a profound impact on students ranging from K-12 through higher education across California and Virginia.
In California, Ryan’s work was funded through the brilliant work and vision of Dr Geri Keskeys and the support of a Warhol Foundation grant, which enabled him to serve as a resident artist-teacher at Elk Grove Elementary, where he taught art and design to students from kindergarten through sixth grade. During this time, he developed a competitive and effective curriculum that rivaled the preexisting standards in California. He also taught as the instructor of record at a private school for grades K through 8 called CourtYard, further honing his educational strategies and curriculum development skills.
Ryan has 30 years dedicated to teaching, enriching the lives of his students, and fostering a dynamic learning environment. His comprehensive background and dedication to art education make him a pivotal figure in academic and artistic communities.
Ryan is the founder and Director of Shockoe Artspace in Richmond, VA, an art gallery that has become a hub for local and nationally recognized artists since its inception. Ryan has exhibited his work widely, including the Virginia Museum of Contemporary Art and the Taubman Museum of Art. His artworks, celebrated for their depth and complexity, have been featured in prestigious publications like New American Paintings and Image Journal. They are cherished parts of private and public collections, such as that of Capital One. His written and visual work and insights were recently highlighted in “A Prophet in the Darkness: Exploring Theology in the Art of Georges Rouault,” published as part of IVP’s Studies in Theology and the Arts series.
Beyond the canvas and classroom, Ryan co-directed The Builder, an award-winning documentary and co-hosted the insightful Shockoe Artspeak podcast, further amplifying his influence in the art world. As a co-founder of The Maker Institute of Studio Art and Theology and Made Makers Christian Art and Design Education and the co-founder of New Christian Makers, the only Christian in-print juried exhibition publication in the world, he and his family work faithfully to reform and redeem art education through a biblically integrated curriculum designed specifically for homeschool families and Christian private schools.
Ryan and his wife, Laura, homeschool their four children, embodying the very principles he teaches. They reside in Richmond, VA, and are active members of Remnant Church, living out their faith in every aspect of life.

name: Sarah Reid
bio: Sarah Reid has been a homeschool mom and private educator for 19 years. She has a BA in Organizational Management and an MA in Writing, she is a lifelong learner, educator, author, and speaker.

name: Scott Lindberg
bio: Scott is a loving husband and father of four homeschooled kids. He has a BS in Systems Engineering from West Point and an MBA from Yale's School of Management. Scott founded Free Market Kids to give the next generation the knowledge and tools to maximize their chances for freedom, success and happiness. He does this by designing tabletop games and courses focused on first principle ideas, e.g., money literacy.

name: Sean Allen
bio:
Sean Allen is a passionate homeschooling advocate, speaker, and founder of The Well Ordered Homeschool, a platform dedicated to supporting parents in their homeschooling journey. A devoted husband to his wife, Caroline, and proud father of eight children, Sean brings a wealth of personal and practical experience to his work. With a Bachelor of Fine Arts in Graphic Design, he combines his creative expertise with a deep commitment to helping families navigate the challenges and joys of homeschooling.

As a speaker at Great Homeschool Conventions, Sean is known for his engaging, heartfelt, and practical presentations that resonate with homeschooling parents. His talks focus on creating organized, effective, and fulfilling homeschool environments, offering strategies to balance education with family life and how to be more effective as a homeschooling parent. Drawing from his own experience raising and educating a large family, Sean provides practical, actionable insights and encouragement, emphasizing the beauty and importance of homeschooling as a lifestyle. His approachable style and relatable anecdotes make him a favorite among audiences seeking inspiration and guidance.

In addition to his speaking engagements, Sean designs resources through The Well Ordered Homeschool to assist parents in streamlining their homeschooling efforts. His work reflects a dedication to fostering strong family bonds and equipping parents with the tools to raise well-educated, confident children. When not speaking or creating, Sean enjoys family time and pursuing his creative interests

name: Sean Ardizzone
bio:
As the founder and CEO of ArkCybr, a leading Cyber Security Service Provider, Sean specializes in making cybersecurity clear, practical, and accessible for families and homeschool communities. Drawing on a background in technical management, military-connected service, and two decades of hands-on experience with enterprise networks, Sean develops educational resources—including the BE SURE framework—focused on helping parents and students master digital safety in everyday life.​
Through national conferences, live lab demonstrations, and the creation of step-by-step cybersecurity courses tailored for homeschoolers, Sean blends technical expertise with a passion for teaching. Families benefit from simple, actionable guidance on topics like privacy, online threats, and safe technology use—empowering them to navigate the digital world with confidence and wisdom.​
Sean is dedicated to equipping the next generation with the knowledge and habits needed for lasting digital resilience, making technology a tool for growth rather than a source of risk.

Sean is a cyber expert, who holds multiple industry certifications including the OSCP, OSWP, C|EH, ITILfV3, Security+ Sean has worked in the federal government contracting industry, for fortune 10 corporations, and is presently the CEO of ArkCybr.

name: Sera Johnson
bio: Sera Johnson is a wife, 21 year homeschooling mother of four, grandmother, founder of Choosing Healthy Life, Inc., co-author of award-winning, nutrition and health curriculum "Nutrition 101: Choose Life!”, international speaker and essential oils and natural health educator. Since 2006, Sera has been empowering families to take control of their health and grasp ahold of God's wellness, purpose and abundance for their lives.

name: Shane Bowles
bio:
Shane Bowles is a composer, performer, and educator passionate about empowering the next generation of music creators. He performed solo at Carnegie Hall and later earned a 5.9 out of 6 teacher rating at Utah State University.
