His career spans arranging and composing across multiple genres, and he has recorded with The Piano Guys and Jenny Phillips.

Shane is a sought-after teacher known for his expertise in improvisation, arranging, and composition. He is the founder of Music Unveiled, a program that trains children to create music from the very beginning of their piano journey—equipping them not just to play notes, but to think, speak, and write the language of music.

name: Shawna Howell
bio: Shawna started her homeschool journey before the internet! Fast forward 30 years, in the spirit of Titus 2 with a passion for helping parents find joy in the journey of a Christian, classical home education, Shawna equips families to discover community integrated within lifelong learning!

name: Sherry Martz
bio:
Sherry found Brain Balance in 2016 because her 13 yo son was struggling with anxiety, depression, and panic attacks. The assessment results showed gaps in development that were causing those struggles. While witnessing the amazing brain shifts in her son she decide she wanted to be a part of Brain Balance and bought the Brain Balance of Cincinnati Center. Her background is in Business Management, and like most Moms with struggling kiddos, she became an expert and now runs the business to help other kiddos and parents too.

Brain Balance of Cincinnati Owner, and Brain Balance Parent

name: Sonya Shafer
bio:
Sonya Shafer is a popular homeschool speaker and writer, specializing in the Charlotte Mason Method. She has been on an adventure for more than 25 years studying, researching, practicing, and teaching Charlotte’s gentle and effective methods of education. Her passion for homeschooling her own four daughters grew into helping others and then into Simply Charlotte Mason, which publishes her many books and provides a place of practical encouragement to homeschoolers at simplycharlottemason.com.

Sonya Shafer is a popular homeschool speaker and writer, specializing in the Charlotte Mason Method. She has been on an adventure for more than 20 years studying, researching, practicing, and teaching Charlotte’s gentle and effective methods of education. Her passion for homeschooling her own four daughters grew into helping others and then into Simply Charlotte Mason, which publishes her many books and provides a place of practical encouragement to homeschoolers at simplycharlottemason.com.

name: Stefanie Hohl
bio:
Stefanie Hohl is the creator of ABC See, Hear, Do, a learn-to-read program that teaches children to read through movement. With a Master’s in Education in Curriculum and Instruction from Penn State and a Master of Fine Arts in Writing for Children and Young Adults from Vermont College of Fine Arts, Stefanie is passionate about discovering effective, engaging ways to teach young children. Her ABC See, Hear, Do books and resources have reached more than 200,000 families and educators worldwide. She loves sharing her knowledge with others through speaking, writing, and collaborating. Stefanie lives in Pittsburgh with her husband, five children, two very silly dogs, and two cuddly cats.

Stefanie Hohl, M.Ed., is the creator of the award-winning ABC See, Hear, Do series. With advanced degrees in both children’s literature and early childhood education, her experience ranges from running preschool co-ops to homeschooling to directing local storytime programs. Stefanie's passion for making learning to read fun for kids (and parents!) not only led her to develop the ABC See, Hear, Do method, but also inspired her to launch Playful Learning Press, a publishing company which specializes in developing movement-based educational resources for young learners. Stefanie lives in Pittsburgh with her husband, five children, a very silly dog, and two ridiculously cute cats.

name: Stephanie Bearce
bio:
Stephanie Bearce is a history detective and a science nerd who loves turning her discoveries into books for kids. She is an award-winning author of more than forty traditionally published nonfiction books as well as the first novel in the Raven Gallows mystery series: The Secret of Moonrise Manor.
Stephanie frequently presents at conferences, schools, literature festivals, and libraries around the country. A winner of the SCBWI Crystal Kite Award and the SCBWI Work in Progress Grant, Stephanie’s books have also been selected for the NSTA Recommended Reading List.
She lives near St. Louis, Missouri, with her family.

name: Stephanie Smith
bio: For over two decades grocery lists and lesson plans dominated Stephanie's life as she raised and educated five sons. She led the start-up of a K-12 homeschool cooperative and served on the founding board and as an instructor of a private Christian school. Today she is a national speaker and author with a passion for building spiritually strong, emotionally healthy, and relationally smart generations. Readers and audiences love her authentic, engaging style and significant (but never stuffy) content. She shares cutting-edge research on human development, insights about human dynamics—what makes people tick—alongside powerful Scriptural truths. Stephanie loves equipping parents with the principles and practices to raise children fully engaged in God's grand story!

name: Stephen Souders
bio:
Stephen Souders is co-creator and co-owner of MYTEK LAB, a Technology education company. Stephen has worked in the field of information technology since he was 15 years old when he began his career by working for an ISP while still in high school. He holds a bachelor's degree in Computer Information Systems from Florida Gulf Coast University and a master's degree in Computer Information Systems from Kennesaw State University. Over the years, Stephen has worked specifically in the web development field, managed an Information Technology department, and taught classes at Georgia Highlands College for over 6 years.
Stephen and his wife Jacky created MYTEK LAB in 2015. Together, Stephen and Jacky not only run and manage MYTEK LAB, they also homeschool both of their teenage daughters from their home state of Florida.

name: Steve Jones
bio: A homeschooling father of two and owner of a plumbing business in Mason, Ohio, Steve Jones has been attending the Great Homeschool Convention for the better part of 20 years. His oldest daughter has successfully graduated and is in her first year of college. Steve's youngest continues to school at home. Out of concern for the welfare of his daughters and future grandchildren, Steve got involved in Convention of States in 2013. Since that time, Steve has been instructing the public and Ohio legislators about the tool we have in Article V of the US Constitution that can improve the federal government by limiting its authority.

name: Suzanne Gose
bio:
Suzanne Gose is a happily married mother of five children, small business owner, as well as a founder & board member of the Community Homeschool Center. A certified Texas Public School Teacher turned homeschooler, Suzanne stayed home with their first born and missed teaching so much, she reached out to our local community to tutor or host a small Spanish class. After that initial email, she received 42 replies the very next morning! So for 22 years now, she has been teaching Spanish and public speaking to homeschool students weekly in a classroom setting, most recently at the Community Homeschool Center in Bryan, Texas.

Those classes turned required developing Flip Flop Spanish! An all age teaching and full family learning curriculum in a method that is TESTED and SUCCESSFUL by thousands of Homeschooling families.

name: Tamra Lanning
bio: With over 22 years of experience in education, Dr. Tamra Lanning is the Director of Customer Success at Learning Hub, an innovative company supporting homeschool families and K–12 students through engaging, adaptive learning experiences. A former educator and school leader, Tamra is passionate about empowering parents to personalize learning and help every child grow at their own pace. At Learning Hub, she leads initiatives that make quality instruction and meaningful progress accessible to all learners.

name: Teri White
bio:
With over 22 years of dedicated service to her family, Teri has been deeply committed to home educating her children and actively participating in the homeschooling community. Following her extensive experience in home education, she transitioned into a role as a Community Health Educator with a life-affirming non-profit organization. In this capacity, Teri has taught sexual risk avoidance education to thousands of students across various educational settings, including elementary, middle, and high school public schools, private Christian schools, and home education environments.
Teri brings a profound understanding of the complex and often contentious nature of sex education in today’s cultural climate. She believes that maintaining moral integrity is essential when addressing this critical topic. Her academic background includes a Bachelor of Science Degree in Community Health Education from Texas A&M University. Additionally, Teri is a Certified Sexual Risk Avoidance (SRA) Specialist and a Certified Life Coach, specializing in sexual health, issues related to sexual trauma, and is the author of Acknowledging Integrity Matters (AIM), a sexual risk avoidance program specifically developed for homeschooling families, co-ops, and churches.
Recognizing that many children, teens, and adults face challenges and pain in this area, Teri approaches her work with compassion. She walks alongside individuals, providing education on sexual integrity and helping them recognize their intrinsic value as image bearers of the Creator.

name: Timothy Shoemaker
bio: Tim Shoemaker is a dynamic Christian author, speaker, and writing coach whose passion for engaging families through creative, faith-based teaching has inspired countless parents and youth. With over 20 books to his name, including the award-winning Code of Silence series (named in Booklist’s “Top Ten Crime Novels for Youth”) and devotional works like Smashed Tomatoes, Bottle Rockets: And Other Outdoor Devotionals You Can Do with Your Kids, Tim specializes in crafting resources that make spiritual lessons memorable and fun. His High Water series with Focus on the Family blends suspense, adventure, and Christian values, resonating deeply with young readers and homeschooling families.

name: Tina Freitas
bio:
Tina Freitas is a devoted Christian, wife, mother, and passionate advocate for homesteading, homeschooling and family values – and co-host of the Making the Argument podcast. Married to her high school sweetheart, Nick Freitas, since 1999, Tina has homeschooled their three children—Lillyana, Luke, and Alexandria—in Culpeper, Virginia. What began as a practical choice due to Nick’s military service as a Green Beret evolved into a deep commitment to fostering independent thinking, strong character, and faith-based education in their children. Tina’s homeschooling journey, grounded in her Christian worldview, emphasizes nurturing children’s unique gifts while equipping them to navigate the world with confidence and conviction.

With a warm and relatable approach, Tina shares practical insights and encouragement for homeschooling parents, drawing from over a decade of experience. As a speaker, she offers guidance on creating a flexible homeschool environment, balancing family life, and empowering children to thrive academically and spiritually. Tina’s perspective complements her husband’s advocacy for liberty and education freedom, making her a trusted voice for families seeking to raise principled, engaged citizens.

As a featured speaker at the 2026 Great Homeschool Conventions in South Carolina, Ohio, California, and Texas, Tina will inspire attendees from 38 states with her heartfelt wisdom on faith, family, and homeschooling. Her sessions will provide practical strategies and encouragement for parents navigating the rewards and challenges of home education. Tina’s dedication to her family and her authentic, faith-centered approach have made her a compelling and well-loved speaker for homeschooling families across the nation.

name: Tori Wilson, M.Ed.
bio: Neuroeducation Director, Certified Cognitive, Academic, & Behavior Coach, Psychometrist, and Certified TeacherTori is a Certified Cognitive, Academic, & Behavior Coach, Psychometrist, and Neuroeducation Director at The Nectar Group. She received her Bachelor of Arts degree in Elementary Education and Masters of Education in Curriculum and Instruction with an emphasis in Literacy Studies from University of Texas Arlington. She loves changing the lives of her students as she watches them grow and has fun helping parents understand the best options for supporting their children. She feels like each student is their own main character and she is grateful to be a part of their story. When not working with students at Nectar or speaking to parents, she enjoying playing the piano, word games, reading, strategy board games, and spending time with her husband and baby girl.

name: Tricia Siletti
bio: Tricia Siletti is an experienced educator, homeschool consultant, and curriculum designer with over 25 years of teaching experience spanning public education, corporate training, herbal studies, and homeschooling. As the founder of From Earth to Sky and EasyHomeschool.org, she empowers parents to become confident, effective educators through hands-on, contextual learning and personalized homeschool guidance. Tricia holds multiple master’s degrees in Curriculum & Instruction, Kinesiology, and Contemporary Alternative Medicine and is passionate about helping families design meaningful, curiosity-driven education that fits their unique values and goals.

name: Troy Hooker
bio: Troy is an author, former high school teacher, and follower of Jesus. When he is not hiking the mountains or exploring lost beaches in his converted school bus, he is authoring new stories. Often you may find him in a hammock in the woods with a laptop and a cup of coffee, dreaming of his next epic adventure. Troy is the author of the Descendants of Light and Demon Tide series, with many more books to come. Through his writing, he is always seeking to bring others to God by using imaginative plots where light overcomes darkness, fantastical worlds abound, and incredible creatures flourish. Troy lives close to a coffee shop in a small town in Michigan with his wife, Stacy.

name: Wendy Ng
bio: Wendy Ng is a wife and “unlikely homeschool mom” who has spent 16 years educating her four children at home. What began as an unexpected calling has become a journey of joy, trust, and sanctification. Active in her local homeschool community, she delights in encouraging families with practical wisdom and reminders of God’s sovereignty and provision. Wendy also serves as a Dual Enrollment Admissions Counselor at Cedarville University, where she guides students and families as they prepare for the next chapter of their educational journey.
